nodes.description: Adds a repository to an existing organization-level GitHub Actions secret that is configured for 'selected' repository access.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_REPO_TO_ORG_SECRET_WITH_SELECTED_VISIBILITY
nodes.name: Add selected repo to org secret
nodes.description: Grants an existing repository access to an existing organization-level Dependabot secret when the secret's visibility is set to 'selected'; the repository must belong to the organization, and the call succeeds without change if access already exists.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_RUNNER_LABELS
nodes.name: Add runner labels
nodes.description: Adds and appends custom labels to a self-hosted repository runner, which must be registered and active.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_SELECTED_REPOSITORY_TO_ORGANIZATION_SECRET
nodes.name: Add selected repository to an organization secret
nodes.description: Adds a repository to an organization secret's access list when the secret's visibility is 'selected'; this operation is idempotent.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_SELECTED_REPOSITORY_TO_ORGANIZATION_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Add selected repository to an organization variable
nodes.description: Grants a repository access to an organization-level GitHub Actions variable, if that variable's visibility is set to 'selected_repositories'.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_SELECTED_REPOSITORY_TO_USER_SECRET
nodes.name: Add a selected repository to a user secret
nodes.description: Grants a specified repository access to an authenticated user's existing Codespaces secret, enabling Codespaces created for that repository to use the secret.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_SOCIAL_ACCOUNTS_FOR_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Add social accounts for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Adds one or more social media links (which must be valid, full URLs for platforms supported by GitHub) to the authenticated user's public GitHub profile.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_STATUS_CHECK_CONTEXTS
nodes.name: Add status check contexts
nodes.description: Adds status check contexts to a protected branch's required status checks. This action appends new status check contexts to the existing list of required status checks for a protected branch. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with status checks configured. Note: The 'contexts' parameter is deprecated by GitHub in favor of 'checks' array. For new implementations, consider using update_status_check_protection with the 'checks' parameter instead.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_SUB_ISSUE
nodes.name: Add sub-issue to an issue
nodes.description: Tool to add a sub-issue to a parent GitHub issue using GraphQL. Use when you need to establish a parent-child relationship between issues. Requires the 'sub_issues' GraphQL feature to be enabled.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_TEAM_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Add team access restrictions
nodes.description: Adds teams to the list of teams with push access to a protected branch. This action grants additional teams push access to a protected branch. Unlike 'Set team access restrictions' (PUT), this action adds to the existing list rather than replacing it entirely. Prerequisites: - The repository must be owned by an organization (not a personal account) - The branch must have protection rules enabled - The branch protection must have restrictions configured - The teams must exist in the organization and have repository access

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_USER_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Add user access restrictions
nodes.description: Adds users to the list of people allowed to push to a protected branch in an organization repository. Important notes: - This action only works on organization-owned repositories (not personal repos) - The branch must already have protection rules with restrictions enabled - Users must have write access to the repository to be added - The combined total of users, apps, and teams is limited to 100 items

nodes.label: GITHUB_ADD_USERS_TO_CODESPACES_ACCESS_FOR_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Add users to codespaces access for an organization
nodes.description: Adds organization members to the list of users granted Codespaces access billed to the organization. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires the organization's Codespaces billing access to be set to 'selected_members' mode. If not already configured, use the GITHUB_MANAGE_ACCESS_CONTROL_FOR_ORGANIZATION_CODESPACES action first to set visibility to 'selected_members'.

nodes.label: GITHUB_API_ROOT
nodes.name: Get GitHub API root
nodes.description: Retrieves a map of all top-level GitHub REST API resource URLs and their templates.

nodes.label: GITHUB_APPROVE_WORKFLOW_RUN_FOR_FORK_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Approve a workflow run for a fork pull request
nodes.description: Approves a workflow run from a forked repository's pull request; call this when such a run requires manual approval due to workflow configuration.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ASSIGN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE_TO_TEAM
nodes.name: Assign an organization role to a team
nodes.description: Assigns an existing organization-level role (identified by `role_id`) to a team (identified by `team_slug`) within a GitHub organization (`org`), provided the organization, team, and role already exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ASSIGN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE_TO_USER
nodes.name: Assign an organization role to a user
nodes.description: Assigns a specific organization role to a user who is a member or an outside collaborator in a GitHub organization, using a valid role ID.

nodes.label: GITHUB_AUTH_USER_DOCKER_CONFLICT_PACKAGES_LIST
nodes.name: List Docker Migration Conflicting Packages
nodes.description: List Docker packages with migration conflicts for the authenticated user. This endpoint lists all Docker packages owned by the authenticated user that encountered namespace conflicts during the Docker-to-GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) migration. Conflicts occur when a package with the same name exists in both the legacy Docker registry and GHCR. IMPORTANT: The Docker registry for GitHub Packages was deprecated on Feb 24, 2025. This endpoint may return a 400 error with message 'Package migration for docker is no longer supported' as the migration period has ended. In this case, the action returns an informative response instead of failing. Use case: Identifying packages that require manual migration to GHCR. Required scope: read:packages (for OAuth and personal access tokens).

nodes.label: GITHUB_BLOCK_USER
nodes.name: Block a user
nodes.description: Blocks an existing individual GitHub user (not an organization or your own account), preventing them from interacting with your account and repositories.

nodes.label: GITHUB_BLOCK_USER_FROM_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Block a user from an organization
nodes.description: Blocks a GitHub user from an organization, preventing their contributions, collaboration, and forking of the organization's repositories. Requires admin or 'Blocking users' organization permission (write access).

nodes.label: GITHUB_CANCEL_GITHUB_PAGES_DEPLOYMENT
nodes.name: Cancel a GitHub Pages deployment
nodes.description: Cancels an existing, ongoing or queued GitHub Pages deployment for a repository using its `pages_deployment_id`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CANCEL_SPONSORSHIP
nodes.name: Cancel sponsorship
nodes.description: Tool to cancel an active GitHub sponsorship using GraphQL. Use when you need to terminate a sponsorship relationship between a sponsor and a sponsorable entity. Either sponsor ID/login and sponsorable ID/login must be provided.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CANCEL_WORKFLOW_RUN
nodes.name: Cancel a workflow run
nodes.description: Cancels a workflow run in a GitHub repository if it is in a cancellable state (e.g., 'in_progress' or 'queued'). Returns 202 Accepted on success.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_IF_GIST_IS_STARRED
nodes.name: Check if a gist is starred
nodes.description: Checks if a gist is starred by the authenticated user. Returns `is_starred: true` if the gist is starred (HTTP 204), or `is_starred: false` if not starred (HTTP 404). Use this to verify star status before starring/unstarring a gist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_IF_PULL_REQUEST_HAS_BEEN_MERGED
nodes.name: Check if pull request merged
nodes.description: Checks if a specified GitHub pull request has been merged, indicated by a 204 HTTP status (merged) or 404 (not merged/found).

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_CAN_BE_ASSIGNED
nodes.name: Check if a user can be assigned
nodes.description: Verifies if a GitHub user can be assigned to issues in a repository; assignability is confirmed by an HTTP 204 (No Content) response, resulting in an empty 'data' field in the response.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_CAN_BE_ASSIGNED_TO_ISSUE
nodes.name: Check if a user can be assigned to an issue
nodes.description: Checks if a specified GitHub user can be assigned to a given issue within a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_FOLLOWS_ANOTHER_USER
nodes.name: Check if a user follows another user
nodes.description: Checks if a GitHub user `username` follows `target_user`; returns a 204 HTTP status if true, 404 if not or if users are invalid.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_IS_BLOCKED_BY_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Check if a user is blocked by the authenticated user
nodes.description: Checks if the specified GitHub user is blocked by the authenticated user; a 204 No Content response indicates the user is blocked, while a 404 Not Found indicates they are not.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_IS_BLOCKED_BY_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Check if a user is blocked by an organization
nodes.description: Checks if a GitHub user is blocked by an organization. Returns is_blocked=True if the user is blocked (HTTP 204), or is_blocked=False if not blocked (HTTP 404). Requires 'Blocking users' organization permission (read access).

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_IS_REPOSITORY_COLLABORATOR
nodes.name: Check if a user is a repository collaborator
nodes.description: Checks if a user is a collaborator on a specified GitHub repository, returning a 204 status if they are, or a 404 status if they are not or if the repository/user does not exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_PERSON_FOLLOWED_BY_AUTH_USER
nodes.name: Check if person is followed by authenticated user
nodes.description: Checks if the authenticated GitHub user follows a target GitHub user. Returns a structured response with is_following=True when following (HTTP 204), or is_following=False when not following (HTTP 404).

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_PRIVATE_VULNERABILITY_REPORTING_STATUS
nodes.name: Check private vulnerability reporting status
nodes.description: Checks if private vulnerability reporting is enabled for the specified repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_PUBLIC_ORGANIZATION_MEMBERSHIP_FOR_USER
nodes.name: Check public organization membership
nodes.description: Tool to check if a user is a public member of an organization. Use when you need to verify public organization membership status.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_TEAM_PERMISSIONS_FOR_A_PROJECT
nodes.name: Check team permissions for a project
nodes.description: Checks if a team has 'read', 'write', or 'admin' permissions for an organization's specific classic project, returning the project's details if access is confirmed.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_TEAM_PERMISSIONS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Check team permissions for a repository
nodes.description: Checks a team's permissions for a specific repository within an organization, including permissions inherited from parent teams.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CHECK_TOKEN
nodes.name: Check a token
nodes.description: Checks if a GitHub App or OAuth access_token is valid for the specified client_id and retrieves its details, typically to verify its active status and grants. NOTE: This endpoint requires Basic Authentication using the OAuth App's client_id as username and client_secret as password. The access_token parameter must be an OAuth token issued by the specified OAuth App.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CLEAR_PROJECT_V2_ITEM_FIELD_VALUE
nodes.name: Clear Project V2 Item Field Value
nodes.description: Tool to clear the value of a field for an item in a GitHub Project V2. Use when you need to remove or reset a field value (text, number, date, assignees, labels, single-select, iteration, or milestone fields).

nodes.label: GITHUB_CLEAR_REPOSITORY_CACHE_BY_KEY
nodes.name: Clear repository cache by key
nodes.description: Deletes GitHub Actions caches from a repository matching a specific `key` and an optional Git `ref`, used to manage storage or clear outdated/corrupted caches; the action succeeds even if no matching caches are found to delete.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CLEAR_SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_ORG_LABELS
nodes.name: Clear self-hosted runner org labels
nodes.description: Removes all custom labels from a self-hosted runner for an organization; default labels (e.g., 'self-hosted', 'linux', 'x64') will remain.

nodes.label: GITHUB_COMMIT_MULTIPLE_FILES
nodes.name: Commit Multiple Files
nodes.description: Tool to atomically create, update, or delete multiple files in a GitHub repository as a single commit. Uses Git Data APIs to avoid SHA mismatch conflicts that occur with the Contents API when multiple files are modified in parallel. Use when you need to make multi-file changes reliably. BRANCH CREATION: When committing to a new branch (e.g., 'fix/my-fix' or 'feature/new-feature'), you MUST provide 'base_branch' (typically 'main' or 'master') to create the branch from. If the branch already exists, base_branch is not needed. This action handles race conditions automatically: if the branch is updated by another commit between fetching the HEAD and updating the reference (resulting in a 422 'not a fast forward' error), the action will retry by refetching the HEAD and rebasing changes. Use max_retries to control this behavior.

nodes.label: GITHUB_COMPARE_TWO_COMMITS
nodes.name: Compare two commits
nodes.description: Compares two commit points (commits, branches, tags, or SHAs) within a repository or across forks, using `BASE...HEAD` or `OWNER:REF...OWNER:REF` format for the `basehead` parameter.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CONFIGURE_JIT_RUNNER_FOR_ORG
nodes.name: Configure JIT runner for an org
nodes.description: Generates a JIT configuration for a GitHub organization's new self-hosted runner to run a single job then unregister; requires admin:org scope and the runner_group_id must exist in the organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CONFIGURE_OIDC_SUBJECT_CLAIM_TEMPLATE
nodes.name: Configure OIDC subject claim template
nodes.description: Sets or updates the OIDC subject claim customization template for an existing GitHub organization by specifying which claims (e.g., 'repo', 'actor') form the OIDC token's subject (`sub`). This action customizes which claim keys are included in the OIDC subject claim for the specified organization. It allows for fine-tuning the content of the subject claim based on organizational needs.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CONVERT_ORG_MEMBER_TO_OUTSIDE_COLLABORATOR
nodes.name: Convert an organization member to outside collaborator
nodes.description: Converts an existing organization member, who is not an owner, to an outside collaborator, restricting their access to explicitly granted repositories.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_BLOB
nodes.name: Create a blob
nodes.description: Creates a Git blob in a repository, requiring content and encoding ('utf-8' or 'base64'). Requires write access to the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_CHECK_RUN
nodes.name: Create a check run
nodes.description: Creates a new check run for a specific commit in a repository, used by external services to report status, detailed feedback, annotations, and images directly within the GitHub UI. NOTE: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication - OAuth tokens and personal access tokens cannot create check runs.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_CHECK_SUITE
nodes.name: Create a check suite
nodes.description: Creates a new check suite for a specific commit (`head_sha`) in an original repository (not a fork). IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a GitHub App installation access token - OAuth tokens and classic personal access tokens cannot use this endpoint. GitHub dispatches a `check_suite` webhook event with the `requested` action upon success.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_CODESPACE_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Create a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for the authenticated user, requiring a JSON request body with either `repository_id` (integer) or a `pull_request` object (containing `pull_request_number` (integer) and `repository_id` (integer)). This action allows users to set up a development environment quickly by creating a codespace from a specified repository or pull request.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_CODESPACE_FROM_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Create a codespace from a pull request
nodes.description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for an open pull request in a Codespaces-enabled repository, with options to customize its configuration.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_CODESPACE_IN_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Create a codespace in a repository
nodes.description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for the authenticated user in a specified repository, which must be accessible and use a valid `devcontainer.json` if `devcontainer_path` is specified.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_COMMIT
nodes.name: Create a commit
nodes.description: Creates a new commit in a GitHub repository; the `tree` SHA and any `parents` SHAs must already exist in the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_COMMIT_COMMENT
nodes.name: Create a commit comment
nodes.description: Creates a comment on a specific commit, or on a specific line if `path` and `position` are provided.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_COMMIT_STATUS
nodes.name: Create a commit status
nodes.description: Sets a commit's status (e.g., error, failure, pending, success from CI/CD) for a given SHA; max 1000 statuses per SHA/context.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_CUSTOM_ORGANIZATION_ROLE
nodes.name: Create a custom organization role
nodes.description: Creates a custom role with defined permissions within a GitHub organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOY_KEY
nodes.name: Create a deploy key
nodes.description: Creates a deploy key for a repository; the repository must exist and be accessible, and the provided key must be a valid public SSH key.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOYMENT
nodes.name: Create a deployment
nodes.description: Creates a GitHub deployment for an existing repository, targeting a specific ref (branch, tag, or SHA) that must also exist within the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOYMENT_BRANCH_POLICY
nodes.name: Create a deployment branch policy
nodes.description: Creates a deployment branch or tag policy for an existing environment in a GitHub repository, using a Ruby File.fnmatch pattern (where `*` doesn't match `/`) to specify which branches or tags are deployable.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOYMENT_STATUS
nodes.name: Create a deployment status
nodes.description: Creates a status for an existing deployment, updating its operational state, associated URLs, and description.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_DISCUSSION
nodes.name: Create a discussion
nodes.description: Creates a new discussion post on a specific team's page within an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_DISCUSSION_COMMENT
nodes.name: Create a discussion comment
nodes.description: Creates a new comment on an existing team discussion within a GitHub organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_FORK
nodes.name: Create a fork
nodes.description: Creates a fork of an accessible repository, optionally into a specific organization, with a new name, or copying only the default branch.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_GIST
nodes.name: Create a gist
nodes.description: Creates a new gist on GitHub with provided files, an optional description, and public/secret visibility.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_GIST_COMMENT
nodes.name: Create a gist comment
nodes.description: Creates a new comment on a specified GitHub gist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_GITHUB_APP_FROM_A_MANIFEST
nodes.name: Create a GitHub App from a manifest
nodes.description: Use this action to complete the GitHub App Manifest flow (step 3 of 3) by exchanging the temporary authorization `code` for the app's full configuration, including credentials and private key. The code is provided via redirect URL after creating the app through GitHub's web interface and must be used within 1 hour.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_GITHUB_PAGES_DEPLOYMENT
nodes.name: Create a github pages deployment
nodes.description: Creates a GitHub Pages deployment for a repository using a specified artifact and OIDC token, provided GitHub Pages is enabled and the artifact (containing static assets) is accessible.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_GITHUB_PAGES_SITE
nodes.name: Create a github pages site
nodes.description: Configures or updates GitHub Pages for a repository, setting build type and source; ensure a Pages workflow exists for 'workflow' `build_type`, or `source_branch` exists for 'legacy' or unspecified `build_type`. This action allows users to effectively manage their GitHub Pages site settings, specifying how the site is built and where the source files are located.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_LABEL
nodes.name: Create a label
nodes.description: Creates a new label in a specified GitHub repository, provided the repository exists and the user has write permissions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_MILESTONE
nodes.name: Create a milestone
nodes.description: Creates a milestone in a GitHub repository for tracking progress on issues or pull requests; requires repository existence and user write permissions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_AN_AUTOLINK_REFERENCE_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Create an autolink reference for a repository
nodes.description: Creates a repository autolink to automatically convert text references (e.g., 'TICKET-123') into hyperlinks, using a unique `key_prefix` and a `url_template` that includes `<num>`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_AN_ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Create an environment variable
nodes.description: Creates an encrypted environment variable for a pre-existing environment within a GitHub repository; will fail if the variable name already exists.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: Create an issue
nodes.description: Creates a new issue in a GitHub repository, requiring the repository to exist and have issues enabled; specific fields like assignees, milestone, or labels may require push access.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_AN_ISSUE_COMMENT
nodes.name: Create an issue comment
nodes.description: Creates a new comment on an existing GitHub issue or pull request within the specified repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_AN_ORGANIZATION_PROJECT
nodes.name: Create an organization project
nodes.description: Creates a new classic project board within a specified GitHub organization. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects (classic) REST API. The classic projects feature may be disabled in some organizations, and GitHub recommends migrating to Projects V2 (accessible via GraphQL API) for new projects. Requirements: - The authenticated user must be an organization member with project creation permissions - Classic projects must be enabled for the organization - Requires 'repo' and 'admin:org' or 'write:org' scopes

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_AN_ORGANIZATION_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Create an organization repository
nodes.description: Creates a new repository within a specified GitHub organization, with options for detailed configuration including visibility, features, merge strategies, initial commit, and templates.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_AN_ORGANIZATION_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Create an organization variable
nodes.description: Creates a new, uniquely named GitHub Actions variable for an organization, with configurable repository access visibility (all, private, or selected).

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_AN_ORGANIZATION_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Create an organization webhook
nodes.description: Creates a webhook for a GitHub organization to deliver event notifications to a configured URL.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_PROJECT_CARD
nodes.name: Create a project card
nodes.description: Creates a project card in a GitHub project column (classic projects only). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) was sunset on August 23, 2024. This API only works with existing classic project columns. New classic projects cannot be created. Consider using GitHub Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead. The request body must contain either: - `note`: A text note for the card - OR `content_id` AND `content_type`: To associate an issue or pull request

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_PROJECT_COLUMN
nodes.name: Create a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Creates a new column in a GitHub project (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Create a pull request
nodes.description: Creates a pull request in a GitHub repository, requiring existing `base` and `head` branches; `title` or `issue` must be provided.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REFERENCE
nodes.name: Create a reference
nodes.description: Creates a NEW Git reference (branch or tag) in a repository. IMPORTANT: This action ONLY creates NEW references - if the reference already exists, it will fail with a 422 'Reference already exists' error. To update an existing reference, use the 'Update a reference' (GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REFERENCE) action instead. The repository must not be empty prior to this operation.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REGISTRATION_TOKEN_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Create a registration token for an organization
nodes.description: Generates a temporary (one-hour) registration token to add a new self-hosted runner to an organization for GitHub Actions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REGISTRATION_TOKEN_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Create a registration token for a repository
nodes.description: Generates a time-limited token required to register a new self-hosted runner with a specific repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_RELEASE
nodes.name: Create a release
nodes.description: Creates a release in a GitHub repository for a specified tag; the tag must be unique for published releases, and if a `discussion_category_name` is given, it must already exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REMOVE_TOKEN_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Create a remove token for an organization
nodes.description: Generates a token, valid for one hour, to authenticate removing a self-hosted runner from an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REMOVE_TOKEN_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Create a remove token for a repository
nodes.description: Generates a temporary (one-hour validity) token required to unregister and remove a self-hosted runner from a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPLY_FOR_A_REVIEW_COMMENT
nodes.name: Create a reply for a review comment
nodes.description: Posts a reply to a specific review comment on a GitHub pull request, requiring the repository, pull request, and original comment to exist, and a non-empty reply body.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPOSITORY_DISPATCH_EVENT
nodes.name: Create a repository dispatch event
nodes.description: Triggers a GitHub Actions workflow or a webhook on a repository by creating a repository dispatch event, allowing programmatic triggering of workflows based on events outside of GitHub.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPOSITORY_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Create a repository for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Creates a new repository for the authenticated user, optionally within an organization if `team_id` is specified.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPOSITORY_FROM_AN_UNPUBLISHED_CODESPACE
nodes.name: Create a repository from an unpublished codespace
nodes.description: Publishes the specified codespace to a new repository, using the codespace's current state as the initial commit.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPOSITORY_PROJECT
nodes.name: Create a repository project
nodes.description: Creates a new GitHub Projects V2 project board linked to a specified repository. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects V1 REST API has been sunset by GitHub. The project is owned by the repository owner (user or organization) and linked to the specified repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPOSITORY_RULESET
nodes.name: Create a repository ruleset
nodes.description: Creates a uniquely named ruleset for a repository, defining rules for branches or tags with specified enforcement, conditions, and bypass actors.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPOSITORY_USING_A_TEMPLATE
nodes.name: Create a repository using a template
nodes.description: Creates a new repository from an existing template repository; the authenticated user must have access to the template and, if creating in an organization, repository creation permissions within it.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPOSITORY_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Create a repository variable
nodes.description: Creates a new, unencrypted variable in a repository for GitHub Actions workflows; fails if a variable with the same name already exists.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPOSITORY_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Create a repository webhook
nodes.description: Creates a webhook for a specified repository; requires admin permissions on the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REVIEW_COMMENT_FOR_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Create a review comment for a pull request
nodes.description: Creates a review comment on a pull request's diff, targeting a specific line, range of lines, an entire file, or replying to an existing comment.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_REVIEW_FOR_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Create a review for a pull request
nodes.description: Creates a pull request review, allowing approval, change requests, or comments; `body` is required if `event` is `REQUEST_CHANGES` or `COMMENT`, and omitting `event` makes the review `PENDING`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_SCOPED_ACCESS_TOKEN
nodes.name: Create a scoped access token
nodes.description: Exchanges a user-to-server token for a new, fine-grained scoped access token for a GitHub App, requiring `client_id`, `access_token`, either `target` or `target_id`, and at least one permission; for repository-specific scoping, provide either `repositories` (names) or `repository_ids` (IDs), but not both.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_SNAPSHOT_OF_DEPENDENCIES_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Create a dependency snapshot
nodes.description: Creates a snapshot of a repository's dependencies to populate the GitHub dependency graph and enable security alerts; `sha` must be a 40-character commit ID, `ref` a fully qualified Git reference (e.g., `refs/heads/main`), and `scanned` an ISO 8601 timestamp.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_TAG_OBJECT
nodes.name: Create a tag object
nodes.description: Creates an annotated Git tag object in a repository, pointing to an existing Git object (commit, tree, or blob) defined by its SHA and ensuring the `type` field correctly specifies the object's type.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_TAG_PROTECTION_STATE_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Create a tag protection state for a repository
nodes.description: DEPRECATED: This API was sunset on August 30, 2024. Use GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPOSITORY_RULESET instead to create tag protection rules via repository rulesets.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_TEAM
nodes.name: Create a team
nodes.description: Creates a new team in an organization, optionally with maintainers, repositories, specific privacy, notification settings, or a parent team; if `parent_team_id` is given, `privacy` must be 'closed'.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_TEMPORARY_PRIVATE_FORK
nodes.name: Create a temporary private fork
nodes.description: Creates a temporary private fork of the specified repository to address a security vulnerability, linking the fork to a GHSA ID that must be specifically associated with this repository; the fork may take up to 5 minutes to become accessible.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_TREE
nodes.name: Create a tree
nodes.description: Creates a new Git tree object in a repository, defining file/directory structure by specifying tree entries, optionally building on a `base_tree` SHA; all provided SHAs must be valid.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_USER_PROJECT
nodes.name: Create a user project
nodes.description: Creates a new GitHub Projects V2 project board for the authenticated user to organize and track issues, pull requests, and notes. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects V1 REST API has been sunset by GitHub.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_A_WORKFLOW_DISPATCH_EVENT
nodes.name: Create a workflow dispatch event
nodes.description: Manually triggers a GitHub Actions workflow identified by `workflow_id` at a given `ref`, if the workflow is configured to accept `workflow_dispatch` events.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_COMMIT_SIGNATURE_PROTECTION
nodes.name: Create commit signature protection
nodes.description: Enables commit signature protection for a specified branch, requiring all new commits to be signed.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_DEPLOYMENT_PROTECTION_RULE
nodes.name: Create a custom deployment protection rule on an environment
nodes.description: Enables a custom deployment protection rule for an existing environment in a repository by linking a configured GitHub App (via `integration_id`) to control deployments.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_DRAFT_ITEM_FOR_USER_PROJECT
nodes.name: Create draft item for user project
nodes.description: Creates a draft issue item in a user-owned GitHub ProjectsV2. Use when you need to add a new draft item to track ideas or tasks in a user's project.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_INFERENCE_CHAT_COMPLETIONS
nodes.name: Create inference chat completions
nodes.description: Tool to create chat completions using GitHub Models inference API. Use when you need to generate AI-powered text responses using models like GPT-4, Claude, or Llama through GitHub's inference endpoint.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_INFERENCE_EMBEDDINGS
nodes.name: Create inference embeddings
nodes.description: Tool to create inference embeddings using GitHub's AI models. Use when you need to convert text into vector representations for similarity search, clustering, or other ML tasks.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_ISSUE_TYPE
nodes.name: Create issue type
nodes.description: Create a new issue type for a GitHub organization. Requires the authenticated user to be an organization administrator. OAuth tokens need admin:org scope.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_JIT_RUNNER_CONFIG
nodes.name: Create JIT runner config for repo
nodes.description: Generates a temporary Just-In-Time (JIT) configuration for a new self-hosted GitHub Actions runner for a repository; any specified non-default `runner_group_id` must be an existing runner group accessible by the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_OR_UPDATE_AN_ENVIRONMENT
nodes.name: Create or update an environment
nodes.description: Creates a new environment or updates an existing one in a GitHub repository, allowing configuration of deployment protection rules such as wait timers and reviewers; ensure `environment_name` is URL-encoded if it contains special characters.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_OR_UPDATE_AN_ENVIRONMENT_SECRET
nodes.name: Create or update an environment secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates an environment secret with an `encrypted_value` that was encrypted using the public key identified by `key_id` for the specified environment.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_OR_UPDATE_AN_ORGANIZATION_SECRET
nodes.name: Create or update an organization secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates an organization secret for GitHub Actions, requiring its value to be pre-encrypted via LibSodium using the organization's public key.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_OR_UPDATE_A_REPOSITORY_SECRET
nodes.name: Create or update a repository secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates a GitHub Actions secret within a specific repository; use `encrypted_value` and `key_id` to set or change its value.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_OR_UPDATE_A_SECRET_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Create or update a secret for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Creates or updates a Codespaces secret for the authenticated user; `encrypted_value` must be encrypted with the public key (ID: `key_id`) from GitHub's 'Get public key for the authenticated user' endpoint.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_OR_UPDATE_CUSTOM_PROPERTIES_FOR_AN_ORG
nodes.name: Create or update custom properties for an organization
nodes.description: Creates new custom property schemas or updates existing ones in bulk for a specified organization; each property definition must include `property_name` and `value_type`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_OR_UPDATE_CUSTOM_PROPERTY_ORG
nodes.name: Create or update a custom property for an organization
nodes.description: Creates a new custom property (name must be unique for creation) or updates an existing one for an organization to define metadata for its repositories.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_OR_UPDATE_FILE_CONTENTS
nodes.name: Create or update file contents
nodes.description: Creates a new file or updates an existing file in a GitHub repository; SHA can be provided to validate file updates, automatically fetched if not provided.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_OR_UPDATE_GITHUB_PAGES_SITE
nodes.name: Create or Update GitHub Pages Site
nodes.description: Tool to enable or update GitHub Pages configuration for a repository. Use when setting up GitHub Pages for static site deployment or modifying Pages settings like custom domains and HTTPS enforcement.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_OR_UPDATE_REPO_SECRET_WITH_ENCRYPTED_VALUE
nodes.name: Create or update repo secret with encrypted value
nodes.description: Creates or updates a Dependabot secret in a repository using an `encrypted_value` (pre-encrypted with LibSodium using the repository's Dependabot public key) and its corresponding `key_id`. This action allows you to securely manage secrets for a given repository, ensuring that sensitive data remains protected during the update process. By providing the encrypted value alongside the key ID, you can seamlessly maintain the necessary credentials for your repository's operations.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_REACTION_FOR_A_COMMIT_COMMENT
nodes.name: Create reaction for a commit comment
nodes.description: Creates an emoji reaction for a commit comment; if the user has already reacted with the same content, details of the existing reaction are returned.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_REACTION_FOR_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: Create reaction for an issue
nodes.description: Creates a reaction for a specified issue within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_REACTION_FOR_AN_ISSUE_COMMENT
nodes.name: Create reaction for an issue comment
nodes.description: Creates a reaction for a specific comment on an issue within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_REACTION_FOR_A_PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW_COMMENT
nodes.name: Create reaction for a pull request review comment
nodes.description: Adds a specified reaction to a pull request review comment within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_REACTION_FOR_A_RELEASE
nodes.name: Create reaction for a release
nodes.description: Creates an emoji reaction for a specific, existing release in a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_REACTION_FOR_A_TEAM_DISCUSSION
nodes.name: Create reaction for a team discussion
nodes.description: Creates a reaction for a team discussion within a GitHub organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_REACTION_FOR_A_TEAM_DISCUSSION_COMMENT
nodes.name: Create reaction for a team discussion comment
nodes.description: Adds a reaction to a team discussion comment, requiring the specified organization, team, discussion, and comment to exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_SPONSORSHIP
nodes.name: Create sponsorship
nodes.description: Tool to start a new GitHub sponsorship or reactivate a past sponsorship using GraphQL. Use when you need to sponsor a maintainer through GitHub Sponsors with a specified amount and privacy level.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_SPONSORS_TIER
nodes.name: Create Sponsors Tier
nodes.description: Tool to create a new payment tier for your GitHub Sponsors profile using GraphQL. Use when you need to add a new sponsorship tier with a specific monthly price and description. The amount is specified in dollars and will be converted to cents automatically.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_UPDATE_ORG_SECRET_WITH_LIB_SODIUM
nodes.name: Create or Update Organization Secret with LibSodium
nodes.description: Creates or updates a Dependabot organization secret, requiring the secret value to be pre-encrypted with LibSodium using the organization's public key obtained from the 'Get an organization public key' endpoint.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_USER_LIST
nodes.name: Create user list
nodes.description: Tool to create a new user list on GitHub. Use when you need to organize and group GitHub users for easier tracking and management.

nodes.label: GITHUB_CREATE_VIEW_FOR_USER_PROJECT
nodes.name: Create view for user project
nodes.description: Tool to create a new view in a user-owned GitHub project (Projects V2). Use when you need to add a customized view with specific layout, filters, and visible fields to organize project items. Note: This action works with GitHub Projects V2. The view can be configured with different layouts (table, board, roadmap) and optional filters. The visible_fields parameter is not applicable to roadmap layouts.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DECLINE_A_REPOSITORY_INVITATION
nodes.name: Decline a repository invitation
nodes.description: Declines a specific, pending repository invitation for the authenticated user, identified by its `invitation_id`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Delete access restrictions
nodes.description: Removes all user, team, and app-based access restrictions from a protected branch.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ADMIN_BRANCH_PROTECTION
nodes.name: Delete admin branch protection
nodes.description: Removes admin enforcement from a protected branch (branch name cannot contain wildcard characters) in a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_FILE
nodes.name: Delete a file
nodes.description: Deletes a file by path from a GitHub repository. The file's blob SHA is automatically fetched if not provided.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Delete a package version for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Deletes an existing package version associated with the authenticated user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_APP_AUTHORIZATION
nodes.name: Delete an app authorization
nodes.description: Deletes an OAuth application grant for a user, which revokes ALL OAuth tokens and their associated authorizations for the application. This endpoint is only accessible to OAuth App owners and requires Basic Authentication using the OAuth App's client_id and client_secret.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_APP_TOKEN
nodes.name: Delete an app token
nodes.description: Revokes a single OAuth access token for an OAuth App or GitHub App with OAuth authorization. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires Basic Authentication using the OAuth App's client_id as username and client_secret as password. It CANNOT be called with personal access tokens, GitHub App installation tokens, or any other token type. You must be the OAuth App owner to use this endpoint.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_REFERENCE
nodes.name: Delete a reference
nodes.description: Deletes a Git reference from a repository; 'ref' must be fully qualified (e.g., 'refs/heads/branch' or 'refs/tags/tag').

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_RELEASE
nodes.name: Delete a release
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specific release, its assets, and potentially its associated Git tag from a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_RELEASE_ASSET
nodes.name: Delete a release asset
nodes.description: Deletes a specific release asset from a GitHub repository; this action is idempotent.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_RELEASE_REACTION
nodes.name: Delete a release reaction
nodes.description: Deletes a reaction from a GitHub release, provided the repository, release, and reaction exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Delete a repository
nodes.description: Permanently deletes the specified repository; this is a destructive, irreversible action that requires admin privileges for the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_REPOSITORY_INVITATION
nodes.name: Delete a repository invitation
nodes.description: Deletes an active repository invitation, permanently revoking a user's access to collaborate on the specified repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_REPOSITORY_RULESET
nodes.name: Delete a repository ruleset
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a repository ruleset.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_REPOSITORY_SECRET
nodes.name: Delete a repository secret
nodes.description: Deletes a named GitHub Actions secret from a specified repository; this operation is destructive and idempotent, and requires the repository to exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_REPOSITORY_SUBSCRIPTION
nodes.name: Delete a repository subscription
nodes.description: Deletes the authenticated user's subscription to a specified repository if it exists, effectively 'unwatching' it.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_REPOSITORY_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Delete a repository variable
nodes.description: Deletes a named variable (e.g., for GitHub Actions workflows) from a repository; the repository and the variable must already exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_REPOSITORY_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Delete a repository webhook
nodes.description: Deletes a specific webhook from a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_REVIEW_COMMENT_FOR_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Delete a review comment for a pull request
nodes.description: Deletes a specific pull request review comment.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ARTIFACT
nodes.name: Delete an artifact
nodes.description: Deletes a GitHub artifact by its ID within a repository, typically resulting in an empty response (HTTP 204 No Content) on success.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_SECRET_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Delete a secret for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Deletes an existing Codespaces secret for the authenticated user by `secret_name`. This is a destructive and irreversible operation. Returns 404 if the secret does not exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_FROM_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Delete a self hosted runner from an organization
nodes.description: Forces the removal of a self-hosted runner from a GitHub organization. This endpoint can be used to completely remove the runner when the machine you were using no longer exists. The runner will be immediately disconnected from GitHub Actions. Requires admin:org scope and organization admin access. Returns 204 No Content on successful deletion.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_FROM_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Delete a self hosted runner from a repository
nodes.description: Removes a specific self-hosted runner (by `runner_id`) from a repository, if registered there; this is idempotent.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_TAG_PROTECTION_STATE_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Delete a tag protection state for a repository
nodes.description: DEPRECATED: This API was sunset on August 30, 2024. Use GITHUB_DELETE_A_REPOSITORY_RULESET instead to manage tag protection rules via repository rulesets.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_TEAM
nodes.name: Delete a team
nodes.description: Deletes a team (and any child teams) from an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_THREAD_SUBSCRIPTION
nodes.name: Delete a thread subscription
nodes.description: Call this to mute a specific notification thread by deleting the user's subscription; notifications may still occur if the user is @mentioned, comments, or due to repository watch settings.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_AUTOLINK_REFERENCE
nodes.name: Delete an autolink reference from a repository
nodes.description: Deletes a specific autolink reference (which automatically links external resource IDs like JIRA-123 to URLs) from the specified repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_A_WORKFLOW_RUN
nodes.name: Delete a workflow run
nodes.description: Deletes a specific workflow run from a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_BRANCH_PROTECTION
nodes.name: Delete branch protection
nodes.description: Removes all protection rules from a specific branch in a GitHub repository; the branch must currently have protection rules enabled.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_CODE_SCANNING_ANALYSIS
nodes.name: Delete a code scanning analysis from a repository
nodes.description: Deletes a specific code scanning analysis by its ID from a repository; `confirm_delete` must be `true` if it's the last analysis of its type for a given tool and reference to prevent data loss.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_CODESPACE
nodes.name: Delete a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Deletes a specific codespace owned by the authenticated user; this is a destructive action and the codespace must exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_COMMIT_COMMENT
nodes.name: Delete a commit comment
nodes.description: Deletes a specific commit comment, identified by its `comment_id`, from the specified repository; the comment must exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_COMMIT_COMMENT_REACTION
nodes.name: Delete a commit comment reaction
nodes.description: Deletes a reaction from a commit comment in a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_COMMIT_SIGNATURE_PROTECTION
nodes.name: Delete commit signature protection
nodes.description: Disables GPG commit signature protection for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, meaning commits pushed to this branch no longer require GPG signing.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_CUSTOM_ORGANIZATION_ROLE
nodes.name: Delete a custom organization role
nodes.description: Deletes a custom organization role (which should not be actively assigned) by its ID; a 204 No Content response indicates success.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_DEPENDEBOT_SECRET_BY_NAME
nodes.name: Delete dependabot secret by name
nodes.description: Deletes a specific Dependabot secret, identified by its name, from a given repository if both the repository and secret exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_DEPLOY_KEY
nodes.name: Delete a deploy key
nodes.description: Deletes a specific deploy key from a repository; to change a key's properties or access scope, the existing key must be deleted and a new one created.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_DEPLOYMENT
nodes.name: Delete a deployment
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specified *inactive* deployment from a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_DEPLOYMENT_BRANCH_POLICY
nodes.name: Delete a deployment branch policy
nodes.description: Deletes a specific deployment branch or tag policy, identified by its ID, from a given environment within a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_DISCUSSION
nodes.name: Delete a discussion
nodes.description: Deletes a specific team discussion, identified by its number, from an organization's team.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_DISCUSSION_COMMENT
nodes.name: Delete a discussion comment
nodes.description: Deletes a specific comment from an existing team discussion within an organization, provided the specified organization, team, discussion, and comment all exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_EMAIL_ADDRESS
nodes.name: Delete authenticated user email address
nodes.description: Sends an empty request body to `DELETE /user/emails` to attempt deletion of user email addresses; the API typically requires specific emails, so outcome may vary.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ENVIRONMENT
nodes.name: Delete an environment
nodes.description: Deletes an existing deployment environment from an existing repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ENVIRONMENT_SECRET
nodes.name: Delete an environment secret
nodes.description: Deletes an existing and accessible secret from a specified environment within a GitHub repository, returning an empty dictionary on success or error details otherwise.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Delete an environment variable
nodes.description: Deletes a named environment variable from a specified, existing environment within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_GIST
nodes.name: Delete a gist
nodes.description: Permanently deletes an existing GitHub gist, specified by its `gist_id`; this action is destructive and cannot be undone.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_GIST_COMMENT
nodes.name: Delete a gist comment
nodes.description: Deletes a specific comment from a GitHub Gist using its `gist_id` and `comment_id`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_GITHUB_ACTIONS_CACHE_BY_ID
nodes.name: Delete GitHub Actions cache by ID
nodes.description: Deletes a specific GitHub Actions cache from a repository using its unique `cache_id`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_GITHUB_PAGES_SITE
nodes.name: Delete a GitHub Pages site
nodes.description: Deletes the GitHub Pages site for the specified repository; completes without error if no site is currently enabled.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ISSUE_COMMENT
nodes.name: Delete an issue comment
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specific comment by its ID from an issue or pull request, if the repository exists and the comment ID is valid.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ISSUE_COMMENT_REACTION
nodes.name: Delete an issue comment reaction
nodes.description: Deletes a reaction from an issue comment in a repository; the repository, comment, and reaction must exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ISSUE_REACTION
nodes.name: Delete an issue reaction
nodes.description: Permanently removes a specific reaction from an issue in a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_LABEL
nodes.name: Delete a label
nodes.description: Permanently removes an existing label from a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_MILESTONE
nodes.name: Delete a milestone
nodes.description: Deletes the specified milestone if it exists; this operation is idempotent, typically returning a 404 if the milestone is not found or already deleted.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Delete an organization
nodes.description: Deletes a GitHub organization and its repositories; this is a destructive action and the organization name will be unavailable for reuse for approximately 90 days.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ORGANIZATION_SECRET
nodes.name: Delete an organization secret
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a secret from a GitHub organization, making it inaccessible to GitHub Actions workflows or other tools.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ORGANIZATION_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Delete an organization variable
nodes.description: Deletes a named GitHub Actions variable from a specified organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ORGANIZATION_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Delete an organization webhook
nodes.description: Deletes a specific webhook, identified by `hook_id`, from an existing organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ORG_CODESPACE
nodes.name: Delete a codespace from the organization
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specific codespace belonging to a member of the specified organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_ORG_PACKAGE
nodes.name: Delete a package for an organization
nodes.description: Deletes a specific package in an organization; cannot delete public packages with over 5,000 downloads.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PACKAGE
nodes.name: Delete a package for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specific package owned by the authenticated user; public packages downloaded over 5,000 times cannot be deleted via this API.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PACKAGE_VERSION
nodes.name: Delete package version
nodes.description: Tool to delete a package version using GitHub GraphQL API. Use when you need to remove a specific version of a package by its global node ID.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Delete package version for an organization
nodes.description: Deletes a specific package version within an organization; requires admin permissions for packages with over 5,000 downloads.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_A_USER
nodes.name: Delete package version for a user
nodes.description: Permanently and irreversibly deletes a specific version of a package owned by the specified user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PENDING_REVIEW
nodes.name: Delete a pending review for a pull request
nodes.description: Deletes a pending (unsubmitted) review from a pull request; this is only possible if the review has not yet been submitted.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PROJECT
nodes.name: Delete a project
nodes.description: Deletes the specified GitHub project (Projects V2) using the GraphQL API. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025 and is no longer available. The project is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. The user must have admin permissions for the project to delete it.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PROJECT_CARD
nodes.name: Delete a project card (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Deletes a project card from a GitHub 'Project (classic)'; this operation is idempotent. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action may return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PROJECT_COLUMN
nodes.name: Delete a project field (column)
nodes.description: Deletes a project field (column) from a GitHub Projects V2 project using the GraphQL API. IMPORTANT: GitHub Classic Projects REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action now uses the GitHub Projects V2 GraphQL API with the deleteProjectV2Field mutation. Note: The built-in Status field cannot be deleted as it is a core field in GitHub Projects V2. Only custom fields can be deleted.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PROJECT_ITEM_FOR_USER
nodes.name: Delete project item for user
nodes.description: Tool to delete a project item for a user in GitHub Projects V2. Use when you need to remove an item from a user's project. Requires the project item's GraphQL node ID (starts with 'PVTI_'). Uses the GitHub GraphQL API deleteProjectV2Item mutation.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PULL_REQUEST_COMMENT_REACTION
nodes.name: Delete a pull request comment reaction
nodes.description: Deletes a specific reaction from a pull request review comment, provided the comment and reaction exist on that comment within the specified repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW_PROTECTION
nodes.name: Delete pull request review protection
nodes.description: Disables the requirement for pull request reviews before merging for a specific, existing branch in an existing repository; this action is idempotent and will succeed even if the protection is not currently enabled.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_REPO_CODESPACE_SECRET_BY_NAME
nodes.name: Delete repo codespace secret by name
nodes.description: Deletes a specific Codespace secret from a repository by its name; this action is idempotent and will succeed even if the secret doesn't exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_SOCIAL_ACCOUNTS_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Delete social accounts for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Deletes currently linked social media account URLs from the authenticated user's GitHub profile.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_TEAM_DISCUSSION_COMMENT_REACTION
nodes.name: Delete team discussion comment reaction
nodes.description: Deletes a reaction from a team discussion comment, given the organization name, team slug, discussion number, comment number, and reaction ID.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_TEAM_DISCUSSION_REACTION
nodes.name: Delete team discussion reaction
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specific reaction from a team discussion within an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_USER_LIST
nodes.name: Delete User List
nodes.description: Tool to delete a GitHub user list using GraphQL. Use when you need to remove a user list from a GitHub account.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_USER_PACKAGE
nodes.name: Delete a package for a user
nodes.description: Deletes a package owned by the specified user, requiring admin permissions for the authenticated user; deletion of public packages with over 5,000 downloads may require GitHub support.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DELETE_WORKFLOW_RUN_LOGS
nodes.name: Delete workflow run logs
nodes.description: Deletes all logs for a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository, provided the repository and run exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DISABLE_A_CUSTOM_PROTECTION_RULE_FOR_AN_ENVIRONMENT
nodes.name: Disable a custom protection rule for an environment
nodes.description: Disables a specific, currently active custom deployment protection rule for an existing environment within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DISABLE_A_WORKFLOW
nodes.name: Disable a workflow
nodes.description: Disables a specified workflow (by ID or filename) in a given GitHub repository, preventing new automatic triggers; any in-progress runs will continue.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DISABLE_PRIVATE_VULN_REPORTING_FOR_REPO
nodes.name: Disable private vulnerability reporting for a repository
nodes.description: Disables private vulnerability reporting for an existing GitHub repository, preventing direct private vulnerability reports to maintainers via GitHub's interface for this repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DISABLE_REPOSITORY_ACTIONS_IN_ORG
nodes.name: Disable Repository Actions In Org
nodes.description: Removes a repository from the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Only works when the organization's `enabled_repositories` policy is set to `selected`. Requires admin:org scope.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DISMISS_A_REVIEW_FOR_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Dismiss a review for a pull request
nodes.description: Dismisses an APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED review on a pull request with a mandatory explanatory message. IMPORTANT: Only reviews in APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED state can be dismissed. Reviews in COMMENTED, PENDING, or already DISMISSED state will return a 422 error. To dismiss a review on a protected branch, you must be a repository admin or be authorized to dismiss pull request reviews.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DOWNLOAD_AN_ARTIFACT
nodes.name: Download an artifact
nodes.description: Downloads a GitHub Actions workflow artifact as a ZIP file. Returns the artifact as a downloadable file. Use GITHUB_LIST_ARTIFACTS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY or GITHUB_LIST_WORKFLOW_RUN_ARTIFACTS to get valid artifact IDs first.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DOWNLOAD_A_REPOSITORY_ARCHIVE_TAR
nodes.name: Download a repository tarball
nodes.description: Downloads a repository's source code as a tarball (.tar.gz) archive for a specific Git reference, if the repository is accessible.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DOWNLOAD_A_REPOSITORY_ARCHIVE_ZIP
nodes.name: Download a repository archive ZIP
nodes.description: Downloads a repository's source code as a ZIP archive for a specific Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA). IMPORTANT SIZE LIMITATION: This action may fail with 'payload too large' errors for large repositories due to platform size restrictions. If you encounter size limit issues, consider these alternatives: - Use GITHUB_GET_REPOSITORY_CONTENT to fetch specific files or directories (also lists directory contents) - Clone the repository using git if you need the full codebase Best suited for: small to medium repositories, downloading specific tagged releases, or quick code inspection.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DOWNLOAD_JOB_LOGS_FOR_A_WORKFLOW_RUN
nodes.name: Download job logs for a workflow run
nodes.description: Downloads logs for a specific job in a GitHub Actions workflow run, contingent on the repository's existence and the job ID being valid and having produced logs.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DOWNLOAD_WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT_LOGS
nodes.name: Download workflow run attempt logs
nodes.description: Downloads a ZIP archive of logs for a specific workflow run attempt. Returns a downloadable ZIP file containing logs for all jobs and steps in the workflow run.

nodes.label: GITHUB_DOWNLOAD_WORKFLOW_RUN_LOGS
nodes.name: Download workflow run logs
nodes.description: Downloads logs for a specific GitHub Actions workflow run as a ZIP archive containing log files for each job in the workflow.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ENABLE_A_WORKFLOW
nodes.name: Enable a workflow
nodes.description: Reactivates a currently disabled GitHub Actions workflow within a repository using its workflow ID or filename.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ENABLE_GITHUB_ACTIONS_IN_SELECTED_REPOSITORIES
nodes.name: Enable GitHub actions in selected repositories
nodes.description: Sets the specific repositories that can use GitHub Actions within an organization by replacing the current list; only applies if the organization's Actions policy is 'selected repositories'.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ENABLE_PRIVATE_VULN_REPORTING_FOR_REPO
nodes.name: Enable private vulnerability reporting for a repository
nodes.description: Enables private vulnerability reporting for a repository, allowing security researchers to privately submit vulnerability reports to maintainers.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ENABLE_REPO_FOR_GITHUB_ACTIONS
nodes.name: Enable repo for Github Actions
nodes.description: Adds a repository to the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Requires the organization's 'enabled_repositories' policy to be set to 'selected'.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ENCRYPT_AND_UPDATE_DEV_SECRET
nodes.name: Encrypt and update dev secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates a repository's development environment secret using an `encrypted_value` and its corresponding `key_id`; the secret must be pre-encrypted with the repository's Codespaces public key.

nodes.label: GITHUB_ENCRYPT_ORG_DEV_ENV_SECRET
nodes.name: Encrypt org dev env secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates an organization's GitHub Codespaces secret using an encrypted value and its corresponding public key ID.

nodes.label: GITHUB_EXPORT_A_CODESPACE_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Export a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Triggers an export of a user's specified codespace, automatically stopping it if active, and returns its export status and download URL.

nodes.label: GITHUB_EXPORT_SBOM_FOR_REPO
nodes.name: Export an SBOM for a repository
nodes.description: Exports the software bill of materials (SBOM) in SPDX JSON format for a repository, if its dependency graph is enabled and it has at least one commit.

nodes.label: GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS
nodes.name: Find Pull Requests
nodes.description: Primary tool to find and search pull requests. Supports filtering by repository, author, state, labels, and merge status, and returns structured PR data for reliable use in workflows. GitHub search results are capped at ~1000 total items; narrow filters when totals approach this limit. `created_since`/`updated_since` filter on creation/update timestamps, not `merged_at`; apply merge-date filtering client-side.

nodes.label: GITHUB_FIND_REPOSITORIES
nodes.name: Find Repositories
nodes.description: AI-optimized repository search with smart filtering by language, stars, topics, and ownership. Builds intelligent search queries and returns clean, actionable repository data. Check `incomplete_results` in the response — when true, results are non-exhaustive. Search endpoints are rate-limited to ~30 requests/minute (vs. 5000/hour for general REST); apply backoff on 403/429. Total results are capped at ~1000 per query regardless of pagination.

nodes.label: GITHUB_FOLLOW_ORGANIZATION_GRAPHQL
nodes.name: Follow Organization (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to follow a GitHub organization using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_FOLLOW_USER
nodes.name: Follow a user
nodes.description: Allows the authenticated user to follow the GitHub user specified by `username`; this action is idempotent and the user cannot follow themselves.

nodes.label: GITHUB_FOLLOW_USER_GRAPHQL
nodes.name: Follow User (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to follow a GitHub user using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow another user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_FORCE_CANCEL_WORKFLOW_RUN
nodes.name: Force cancel a workflow run
nodes.description: Forcefully cancels a queued or in-progress GitHub Actions workflow run, bypassing conditions like always() that would otherwise continue execution. Only use when the standard cancel endpoint fails. Cannot cancel completed workflow runs. Requires write permissions to the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_FORK_GIST
nodes.name: Fork a gist
nodes.description: Forks a specified public gist, creating a copy under the authenticated user's account.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GENERATE_RELEASE_NOTES
nodes.name: Generate release notes content for a release
nodes.description: Generates Markdown release notes content (listing changes, pull requests, and contributors) for a GitHub repository release, customizable via tags and a configuration file.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_BLOB
nodes.name: Get a blob
nodes.description: Retrieves the raw, typically Base64-encoded, content of a file (blob) from a GitHub repository using its SHA hash, if the repository and blob SHA exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_BRANCH
nodes.name: Get a branch
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specified branch within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Get access restrictions
nodes.description: Lists users, teams, and GitHub Apps with push access to a branch; this branch must be protected in repository settings for detailed restrictions, otherwise expect a 404 or empty response.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_COMMIT
nodes.name: Get a commit
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific commit from a repository by its owner, name, and a valid commit reference (SHA, branch, or tag), supporting pagination for large diffs.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_ADMIN_BRANCH_PROTECTION
nodes.name: Get admin branch protection
nodes.description: Checks if repository administrators are subject to the branch protection rules on a specific branch.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_ALL_API_VERSIONS
nodes.name: Get all API versions
nodes.description: Retrieves all officially supported, date-based (e.g., "2022-11-28") versions of the GitHub REST API from the /versions endpoint.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_ALL_CONTRIBUTOR_COMMIT_ACTIVITY
nodes.name: Get all contributor commit activity
nodes.description: Retrieves commit activity (total commits, weekly additions/deletions/commits) for all contributors to a repository; may require a retry if GitHub returns 202 while preparing data.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_ALL_DEPLOYMENT_PROTECTION_RULES_FOR_ENV
nodes.name: Get all deployment protection rules for an environment
nodes.description: Lists all enabled custom deployment protection rules for a specific environment in a repository; the environment must exist and be configured for deployments.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_MILESTONE
nodes.name: Get a milestone
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific milestone within a GitHub repository by its number.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_APP
nodes.name: Get an app
nodes.description: Retrieves publicly available information for an existing GitHub App, identified by its unique URL-friendly `app_slug`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ARTIFACT
nodes.name: Get an artifact
nodes.description: Gets a specific artifact for a repository by `artifact_id`. This action retrieves the details of a specific artifact from a GitHub repository using the artifact's unique identifier. It allows users to access information about artifacts stored in the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ASSIGNMENT
nodes.name: Get an assignment
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub Classroom assignment if the authenticated user is an administrator of the classroom.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_AUTOLINK_REFERENCE_OF_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Get an autolink reference of a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific autolink reference (which automatically hyperlinks text like 'JIRA-123' to an external system) for a repository using its unique ID; requires administrator access to the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ENVIRONMENT
nodes.name: Get an environment
nodes.description: Retrieves the details of a specific deployment environment for a given repository, including its name, configurations, and current status.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ENVIRONMENT_PUBLIC_KEY
nodes.name: Get an environment public key
nodes.description: Retrieves the public key for a specified GitHub repository environment, used to encrypt secrets for GitHub Actions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ENVIRONMENT_SECRET
nodes.name: Get an environment secret
nodes.description: Retrieves metadata (name and timestamps) for a single secret in a GitHub Actions environment. Note: The actual secret value is never returned by this API for security reasons.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Get an environment variable
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific environment variable from a GitHub Actions environment by repository owner, repository name, environment name, and variable name.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: Get an issue
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific issue in a repository using the owner, repository name, and issue number.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ISSUE_COMMENT
nodes.name: Get an issue comment
nodes.description: Action to get an issue comment.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ISSUE_EVENT
nodes.name: Get an issue event
nodes.description: Fetches details of a specific issue event for a given repository and event ID.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Get an organization
nodes.description: Get an organization

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ORGANIZATION_PUBLIC_KEY
nodes.name: Get an organization public key
nodes.description: Action for `GetAnOrganizationPublicKey`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE
nodes.name: Get an organization role
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific GitHub organization role by its ID.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ORGANIZATION_SECRET
nodes.name: Get an organization secret
nodes.description: Gets an organization secret's metadata (e.g., name, creation/update dates, visibility), but not its encrypted value.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ORGANIZATION_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Get an organization variable
nodes.description: Retrieves details (name, value, visibility, timestamps) of a specific, existing variable for an existing GitHub organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_AN_ORGANIZATION_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Get an organization webhook
nodes.description: Retrieves the full configuration, including subscribed events and delivery settings, for an existing organization webhook.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_PACKAGE_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Get a package for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific package (by type and name) from an organization, if both the package and organization exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_PACKAGE_FOR_A_USER
nodes.name: Get a package for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves metadata for a specific package owned by a GitHub user, using package type, name, and username as identifiers.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_PACKAGE_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Get a package for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific package owned by the authenticated user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Get a package version for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific version of a package within an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_A_USER
nodes.name: Get a package version for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific public package version associated with a GitHub user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Get authenticated user package version
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for an existing specific package version associated with the authenticated user, identified by its type, name, and version ID.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_PROJECT
nodes.name: Get a project
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub project (V2) using its project number and owner. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 REST API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_PROJECT_CARD
nodes.name: Get a project card
nodes.description: Retrieves all details of a specific project card, given its unique `card_id`. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action only works with existing classic project cards that were created before the sunset date. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_PROJECT_COLUMN
nodes.name: Get a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific project column from GitHub Projects (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. However, it may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects are enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ Returns column details including: id, name, url, project_url, cards_url, node_id, created_at, and updated_at timestamps.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Get a pull request
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific pull request from a GitHub repository using its owner, repository name, and pull request number.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_REFERENCE
nodes.name: Get a reference
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific Git reference (e.g., a branch, tag, or fully qualified like 'heads/main') from a GitHub repository. The reference must exist in the repository; use 'list_matching_references' first if unsure what references are available.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_RELEASE
nodes.name: Get a release
nodes.description: Gets a specific release from a GitHub repository, provided the repository is accessible and the release exists.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_RELEASE_ASSET
nodes.name: Get a release asset
nodes.description: Gets metadata for a specific release asset in a GitHub repository, including a `browser_download_url` for downloading the asset.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_RELEASE_BY_TAG_NAME
nodes.name: Get a release by tag name
nodes.description: Gets a release from a GitHub repository by its tag name; the repository and a release with this tag must already exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_GET_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Get a repository
