nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORIES_STARRED_BY_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: List repositories starred by the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists repositories the authenticated user has starred, optionally sorted and paginated, including star creation timestamps via 'application/vnd.github.star+json' media type.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORIES_WATCHED_BY_A_USER
nodes.name: List repositories watched by a user
nodes.description: Lists repositories a given GitHub user is watching; the username must be a valid and existing GitHub user handle.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORIES_WATCHED_BY_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: List repositories watched by the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists repositories the authenticated user is watching (subscribed to for notifications). This action retrieves a list of repositories that the authenticated user is currently watching. It allows the user to manage their watched repositories effectively.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORIES_WITH_GITHUB_ACTIONS_CACHE_USAGE
nodes.name: List repos with ghactions cache usage
nodes.description: Retrieves a paginated list of GitHub Actions cache usage statistics for repositories within an existing GitHub organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_ACTIVITIES
nodes.name: List repository activities
nodes.description: Lists activities for a GitHub repository, ensuring the repository exists and is accessible.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_AUTOLINKS
nodes.name: Get all autolinks of a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves all autolinks (e.g., for JIRA issues) configured for a repository; requires admin permissions on the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_COLLABORATORS
nodes.name: List repository collaborators
nodes.description: Lists collaborators for a specified repository, provided it exists and is accessible to the authenticated user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_CONTRIBUTORS
nodes.name: List repository contributors
nodes.description: Lists contributors to a specified repository, sorted by number of contributions in descending order; the repository must exist and be accessible.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_EVENTS
nodes.name: List repository events
nodes.description: Lists chronological events (e.g., code pushes, issue activities, PR actions, forks) for a specified, existing GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_INVITATIONS
nodes.name: List repository invitations
nodes.description: Retrieves all pending (unaccepted or undeclined) collaboration invitations for a specified GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_ISSUES
nodes.name: List repository issues
nodes.description: Lists issues (including pull requests) from a GitHub repository. Requires BOTH 'owner' AND 'repo' parameters (e.g., owner='octocat', repo='Hello-World' for github.com/octocat/Hello-World).

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_LANGUAGES
nodes.name: List repository languages
nodes.description: Lists the programming languages used in a GitHub repository, returning a byte count for each language.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_ORGANIZATION_SECRETS
nodes.name: List repository organization secrets
nodes.description: Lists names of organization-level secrets shared with the specified repository; actual secret values are not returned and visibility depends on token access.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_ORGANIZATION_VARIABLES
nodes.name: List repository organization variables
nodes.description: Lists organization-level variables that are shared with a specific repository. The repository must belong to an organization (not a personal account).

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_PROJECTS
nodes.name: List repository projects
nodes.description: Lists Projects (classic) associated with a specific repository. Note: This action lists GitHub Projects (classic), which is GitHub's legacy project management feature. If the repository has Projects (classic) disabled or uses the newer Projects V2, this will return an empty list. Most modern repositories use Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead of Projects (classic).

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_RULESETS
nodes.name: Get all repository rulesets
nodes.description: Retrieves all rulesets for a GitHub repository, which define conditions and actions for repository interactions (e.g., branch protections).

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_RULE_SUITES
nodes.name: List repository rule suites
nodes.description: Lists rule suite evaluations for a repository, allowing filtering by ref (non-wildcard), time period, actor, and result.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_SECRETS
nodes.name: List repository secrets
nodes.description: Lists metadata for all secrets in a GitHub repository, excluding their encrypted values.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_SECRETS_WITHOUT_DECRYPTING
nodes.name: List repository secrets without decrypting
nodes.description: Lists metadata (e.g., name, creation/update dates) for all Dependabot secrets in a repository; secret values are not included.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_SECURITY_ADVISORIES
nodes.name: List repository security advisories
nodes.description: Tool to list security advisories for a repository. Use when you need to retrieve security advisories published for a specific repository, optionally filtered by state.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_TAGS
nodes.name: List repository tags
nodes.description: Lists tags for a specified GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_TEAMS
nodes.name: List repository teams
nodes.description: Lists all teams with explicit permission to access the specified repository; the repository must exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_TOPICS
nodes.name: Get all repository topics
nodes.description: Retrieves all topics for a specified, existing, and accessible repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_VARIABLES
nodes.name: List repository variables
nodes.description: Lists plain text key-value variables for GitHub Actions workflows within a specific, accessible repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_WEBHOOKS
nodes.name: List repository webhooks
nodes.description: Retrieves a list of webhooks for a repository, which must exist and be accessible.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REPOSITORY_WORKFLOWS
nodes.name: List repository workflows
nodes.description: Lists all GitHub Actions workflows for a specified repository, which must exist and be accessible.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REQUESTED_REVIEWERS_FOR_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Get all requested reviewers for a pull request
nodes.description: Gets all users and teams requested to review a specific pull request in a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REVIEW_COMMENTS_IN_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: List review comments in a repository
nodes.description: Lists all review comments on all pull requests within a specified repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REVIEW_COMMENTS_ON_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: List review comments on a pull request
nodes.description: Lists all review comments on a specific pull request within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_REVIEWS_FOR_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: List reviews for a pull request
nodes.description: Lists submitted reviews chronologically for a specific pull request within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_RUNNER_APPLICATIONS_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: List runner applications for an organization
nodes.description: Lists downloadable GitHub Actions runner application binaries for setting up self-hosted runners in a GitHub organization. Returns download URLs and checksums for various OS/architecture combinations (linux/win/osx on x64/arm/arm64). Requires admin:org scope or fine-grained 'Self-hosted runners' read permission.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_RUNNER_APPLICATIONS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: List runner applications for a repository
nodes.description: Lists available self-hosted runner application binaries for a specific repository, including their OS, architecture, and download URL.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SECRET_SCANNING_ALERTS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: List secret scanning alerts for a repository
nodes.description: List secret scanning alerts for a GitHub repository using first-party detections. Use this to retrieve detected secrets with filtering by state, type, resolution, and validity. Supports optional hiding of secret literals for security.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SECRETS_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: List secrets for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists all Codespaces secrets accessible to the authenticated user for use within GitHub Codespaces.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SECURITY_ADVISORIES_GRAPHQL
nodes.name: List security advisories (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to list GitHub Security Advisories using the GraphQL API. Use when you need to query security vulnerabilities and malware advisories from the GitHub Advisory Database with advanced filtering and pagination options.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SECURITY_VULNERABILITIES
nodes.name: List security vulnerabilities
nodes.description: Query software vulnerabilities documented by GitHub Security Advisories using GraphQL. Use this to find security issues in packages across ecosystems like npm, pip, maven, etc.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SELECTED_REPOSITORIES_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION_SECRET
nodes.name: List selected repositories for a Codespaces organization secret
nodes.description: Lists repositories that have been granted access to a Codespaces organization secret. Returns all repositories that have been selected when the visibility for repository access to a Codespaces secret is set to 'selected'. Requires admin:org scope and admin access to the organization. Note: This endpoint only returns results when the secret's visibility is set to 'selected'. Secrets with visibility 'all' or 'private' do not have specific repository selections.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SELECTED_REPOSITORIES_FOR_A_USER_SECRET
nodes.name: List selected repositories for a user secret
nodes.description: Lists repositories that have access to the specified user Codespaces secret. This endpoint retrieves all repositories that the authenticated user has granted access to for a specific user-level Codespaces secret. The secret must already exist for this endpoint to work. Use 'list_secrets_for_the_authenticated_user' to discover available secrets first. Note: The authenticated user must have Codespaces access enabled. OAuth tokens and personal access tokens require the 'codespace' or 'codespace:secrets' scope.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SELECTED_REPOSITORIES_FOR_SECRET_ACCESS
nodes.name: List selected repos for secret access
nodes.description: Lists repositories within a specified organization that have been granted access to a particular Dependabot secret. Note: This endpoint only returns results when the secret's visibility is set to 'selected'. Secrets with visibility 'all' or 'private' do not have specific repository selections. Requires 'admin:org' scope and the user must have admin access to the organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SELECTED_REPOS_ORG_VARIABLE
nodes.name: List selected repositories for an organization variable
nodes.description: Lists repositories in an organization that can access a specific organization variable; supports pagination and returns an empty list if no repositories have been granted access. Note: This endpoint only works for variables with visibility set to 'selected' - attempting to use it on variables with 'all' or 'private' visibility will result in a 409 Conflict error.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SELF_HOSTED_RUNNERS_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: List self hosted runners for an organization
nodes.description: Lists self-hosted runners for a GitHub organization, optionally filtering by name and paginating results, providing details for each runner such as OS, status, and labels.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SELF_HOSTED_RUNNERS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: List self hosted runners for a repository
nodes.description: Lists all self-hosted runners configured for a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SOCIAL_ACCOUNTS_FOR_A_USER
nodes.name: List social accounts for a user
nodes.description: Lists social media accounts publicly linked to an existing GitHub user's profile.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SOCIAL_ACCOUNTS_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: List social accounts for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists all social media accounts linked to the authenticated user's GitHub profile. This action retrieves the social media accounts associated with the user's GitHub profile, allowing for easy management and visibility of connected accounts. The results include accounts such as Twitter, LinkedIn, and other platforms linked to the GitHub profile.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SPONSORABLES
nodes.name: List Sponsorable Users and Organizations
nodes.description: Tool to list users and organizations who can be sponsored via GitHub Sponsors. Use when you need to discover sponsorable entities with optional filtering by dependency ecosystem.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SSH_SIGNING_KEYS_FOR_A_USER
nodes.name: List SSH signing keys for user
nodes.description: Lists the public SSH signing keys for a specified GitHub user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_STARGAZERS
nodes.name: List stargazers
nodes.description: Lists users who have starred the specified GitHub repository, which must exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_STARRED_GISTS
nodes.name: List starred gists
nodes.description: Retrieves a list of gists starred by the authenticated user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_STATUS_CHECK_CONTEXTS
nodes.name: Get all status check contexts
nodes.description: Fetches all required status check contexts for a protected branch; returns an empty list if the branch isn't protected or has no required checks.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SUB_ISSUES
nodes.name: List sub-issues for an issue
nodes.description: Tool to list sub-issues for a specific GitHub issue. Use when you need to retrieve all sub-issues associated with a parent issue, supporting pagination for large hierarchies.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SUBSCRIPTIONS_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: List marketplace subscriptions for authenticated user
nodes.description: Tool to list GitHub Marketplace subscriptions for the authenticated user. Use when you need to retrieve active marketplace app subscriptions including billing details, plan information, and trial status. Returns subscription information with account, plan, billing cycle, and pricing details.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_SUBSCRIPTIONS_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER_STUBBED
nodes.name: List stubbed subscriptions for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists the authenticated user's stubbed (test/example data, not live) GitHub Marketplace subscriptions, useful for development or testing.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_TAG_PROTECTION_STATES_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: List tag protection states for a repository
nodes.description: DEPRECATED: Lists tag protection rules for a repository. This API was sunset on August 30, 2024. Use GITHUB_GET_ALL_REPOSITORY_RULESETS instead to view tag protection rules via repository rulesets.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_TEAM_MEMBERS
nodes.name: List team members
nodes.description: Lists members of a specific team within an organization, including members of child teams.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_TEAM_PROJECTS
nodes.name: List team projects
nodes.description: Lists GitHub projects accessible to a specific team within an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_TEAM_REPOSITORIES
nodes.name: List team repositories
nodes.description: Lists repositories accessible to a specific team within a GitHub organization. Returns a list of repositories that the specified team has access to, including the team's permission level (admin, maintain, push, triage, pull) for each repository. Requires read:org scope and membership in the organization to access team data.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_TEAMS
nodes.name: List teams
nodes.description: Lists teams for a specified GitHub organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_TEAMS_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: List teams for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists all teams across all organizations to which the authenticated user belongs, supporting pagination.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_TEAMS_THAT_ARE_ASSIGNED_TO_AN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE
nodes.name: List teams assigned to an organization role
nodes.description: Lists teams assigned to a specific role within a GitHub organization. This endpoint requires organization administrator privileges. Use this to audit which teams have been granted specific organization-level permissions through roles. Note: Organization roles is a feature for enterprise-level GitHub organizations.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_THE_PEOPLE_A_USER_FOLLOWS
nodes.name: List the people a user follows
nodes.description: Lists GitHub users that a valid GitHub `username` is following, supporting pagination.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_THE_PEOPLE_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER_FOLLOWS
nodes.name: List people the authenticated user follows
nodes.description: Lists people the authenticated user follows.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_TIMELINE_EVENTS_FOR_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: List timeline events for an issue
nodes.description: Lists chronological events (e.g., comments, commits, label changes) for a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_TOKEN_ACCESS_REPOSITORIES
nodes.name: List token access repositories
nodes.description: Lists repositories in an organization that a fine-grained personal access token has access to. Requires organization admin permissions or 'Personal access tokens' organization read permission. Only GitHub Apps can use this endpoint.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_USER_PROJECTS
nodes.name: List user projects
nodes.description: Retrieves a list of projects for a specified GitHub user, optionally filtering by state and supporting pagination; the username must be a valid GitHub handle. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects V1 REST API has been sunset by GitHub.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_USERS
nodes.name: List users
nodes.description: Retrieves all GitHub users (individuals and organizations) in chronological order of their sign-up date.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_USERS_BLOCKED_BY_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: List users blocked by an organization
nodes.description: Lists users blocked by a specified GitHub organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_USERS_BLOCKED_BY_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: List users blocked by the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists users blocked by the authenticated user, returning an empty list if no users are blocked.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_USERS_THAT_ARE_ASSIGNED_TO_AN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE
nodes.name: List users assigned org role
nodes.description: Lists users assigned to a specific role within a GitHub organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_WATCHERS
nodes.name: List watchers
nodes.description: Retrieves a list of users watching a specific repository; the repository must be accessible to the authenticated user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_WORKFLOW_RUN_ARTIFACTS
nodes.name: List workflow run artifacts
nodes.description: Lists artifacts (e.g., build outputs, test results) for a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_WORKFLOW_RUNS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: List workflow runs for a repository
nodes.description: Lists workflow runs for a repository, allowing filtering by actor, branch, event, status, creation date, check suite ID, or head SHA; the repository must exist and be accessible.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LIST_WORKFLOW_RUNS_FOR_A_WORKFLOW
nodes.name: List workflow runs for a workflow
nodes.description: Lists runs for a specified, existing workflow (identified by ID or filename like `main.yml`) in a GitHub repository, with filtering options.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LOCK_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: Lock an issue
nodes.description: Locks an existing GitHub issue's conversation, preventing further comments; an optional reason can be specified.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LOOKUP_ENTERPRISE_ADMINISTRATOR_INVITATION
nodes.name: Lookup Enterprise Administrator Invitation
nodes.description: Tool to look up a pending enterprise administrator invitation by invitee, enterprise, and role. Use when you need to check if a specific user has a pending invitation to become an enterprise administrator.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LOOKUP_GITHUB_RESOURCE
nodes.name: Lookup GitHub Resource
nodes.description: Tool to lookup a GitHub resource by its URL. Use when you need to identify or retrieve information about a GitHub resource from its URL.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LOOKUP_NODES_BY_IDS
nodes.name: Lookup nodes by IDs
nodes.description: Tool to lookup GitHub nodes by their global node IDs. Use when you need to retrieve multiple objects by their IDs in a single batch query.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LOOKUP_ORGANIZATION_GRAPH_QL
nodes.name: Lookup Organization (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to lookup a GitHub organization by login using GraphQL. Use when you need organization information via GraphQL API.

nodes.label: GITHUB_LOOKUP_TOPIC_BY_NAME
nodes.name: Look Up Topic by Name
nodes.description: Tool to look up a GitHub topic by name using GraphQL. Use when you need to find information about a specific GitHub topic including its popularity (stargazer count) and related topics.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MANAGE_ACCESS_CONTROL_FOR_ORGANIZATION_CODESPACES
nodes.name: Manage access control for organization codespaces
nodes.description: Sets the Codespaces access control policy for a GitHub organization, determining which members can use them. This endpoint requires admin permissions on the organization. The API returns HTTP 204 No Content on success.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MANAGE_CUSTOM_PROPERTIES_FOR_ORG_REPOS
nodes.name: Manage custom properties for org repos
nodes.description: Creates or updates values for an organization's predefined custom properties across multiple repositories (up to 30).

nodes.label: GITHUB_MANAGE_SECRETS_IN_SELECTED_REPOSITORIES
nodes.name: List selected repositories for a GitHub Actions organization secret
nodes.description: Lists repositories within an organization that have been explicitly granted access to a specific GitHub Actions organization secret. Returns all repositories that have been selected when the visibility for repository access to an Actions secret is set to 'selected'. Requires admin:org scope and admin access to the organization. Note: This endpoint only returns results when the secret's visibility is set to 'selected'. Secrets with visibility 'all' or 'private' do not have specific repository selections.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MAP_A_COMMIT_AUTHOR
nodes.name: Map a commit author
nodes.description: Updates Git author information (name and/or email) for an `author_id` obtained during a repository import, to correctly attribute commits. NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MARK_A_THREAD_AS_DONE
nodes.name: Mark a thread as done
nodes.description: Marks the GitHub notification thread (identified by `thread_id`) as done or read for the authenticated user, effectively archiving it.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MARK_A_THREAD_AS_READ
nodes.name: Mark a thread as read
nodes.description: Marks an existing GitHub notification thread, identified by its `thread_id`, as read.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MARK_NOTIFICATIONS_AS_READ
nodes.name: Mark notifications as read
nodes.description: Marks notifications as read or unread, optionally for those updated at or before a `last_read_at` timestamp.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MARK_REPOSITORY_NOTIFICATIONS_AS_READ
nodes.name: Mark repository notifications as read
nodes.description: Marks notifications in a repository as read; if 'last_read_at' is specified, notifications updated after this timestamp are not marked as read.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MERGE_A_BRANCH
nodes.name: Merge a branch
nodes.description: Merges a head branch or commit SHA into a base branch in a repository; fails if there are merge conflicts requiring manual resolution.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MERGE_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Merge a pull request
nodes.description: Merges an open and mergeable pull request in a repository. A 405 error can occur for multiple reasons: (1) The PR is still in draft mode - convert it to ready-for-review first using GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST. (2) Branch protection rules are not satisfied - check for required approving reviews, status checks, or other repository rules that must be met before merging. (3) The base branch has been modified since the PR was last synced - update the PR branch first using GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST_BRANCH.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MOVE_A_PROJECT_CARD
nodes.name: Move a project card (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Moves a project card to a specified position within a column (GitHub Classic Projects). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. It may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects remain enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ On success, the API returns HTTP 201 with an empty body, indicating the card was moved.

nodes.label: GITHUB_MOVE_A_PROJECT_COLUMN
nodes.name: Move a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Moves a column within a GitHub project (classic) to a new position. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. The `position` parameter can be: - 'first': Move the column to the beginning of the project - 'last': Move the column to the end of the project - 'after:<column_id>': Place the column after the specified column (e.g., 'after:67890') See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

nodes.label: GITHUB_PING_AN_ORGANIZATION_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Ping an organization webhook
nodes.description: Sends a 'ping' event to a specified, existing organization webhook to test its configuration and ensure it correctly receives events.

nodes.label: GITHUB_PING_A_REPOSITORY_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Ping a repository webhook
nodes.description: Pings an existing webhook on a repository to test its configuration and reachability by GitHub.

nodes.label: GITHUB_PRIVATELY_REPORT_A_SECURITY_VULNERABILITY
nodes.name: Privately report a security vulnerability
nodes.description: Privately reports a security vulnerability to a repository's maintainers. IMPORTANT: You cannot report vulnerabilities to repositories you own - this API is for third-party security researchers to report to other repositories. The target repository must have private vulnerability reporting enabled. Specify EITHER `severity` OR `cvss_vector_string`, but not both.

nodes.label: GITHUB_PUBLISH_SPONSORS_TIER_GRAPH_QL
nodes.name: Publish Sponsors Tier (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to publish a draft GitHub Sponsors tier using GraphQL. Use when you need to make a draft sponsors tier visible to potential sponsors. The tier must already exist in draft status.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REDELIVER_A_DELIVERY_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Redeliver a delivery for an organization webhook
nodes.description: Redelivers a specific webhook delivery for a webhook within an organization, to resend an event that previously failed or was not processed.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REDELIVER_A_DELIVERY_FOR_A_REPOSITORY_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Redeliver a delivery for a repository webhook
nodes.description: Redelivers a specific, previously made webhook delivery (`delivery_id`) for a repository's webhook (`hook_id`).

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_A_CUSTOM_PROPERTY_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Remove a custom property for an organization
nodes.description: Deletes a custom property, specified by `custom_property_name`, from an existing organization (`org`) for which the property is currently defined, preventing its future assignment to repositories.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_A_LABEL_FROM_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: Remove a label from an issue
nodes.description: Removes a label currently applied to a specific issue in a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_ALL_LABELS_FROM_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: Remove all labels from an issue
nodes.description: Removes all labels from a specified issue in a GitHub repository; this operation is idempotent.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_ALL_ORGANIZATION_ROLES_FOR_A_TEAM
nodes.name: Remove all organization roles for a team
nodes.description: Revokes all organization roles for a team in an organization; this is destructive and succeeds even if the team initially has no roles.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_ALL_ORGANIZATION_ROLES_FOR_A_USER
nodes.name: Remove all organization roles for a user
nodes.description: Revokes all assigned organization-level roles from a specified user (who must be a member of the organization) within a GitHub organization, without removing the user from the organization or affecting repository-specific roles.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_AN_ORGANIZATION_MEMBER
nodes.name: Remove an organization member
nodes.description: Removes a member from a GitHub organization. This action permanently removes a user's membership from the specified organization, revoking their access to all organization resources, repositories, and teams. Requirements: - The authenticated user must be an organization owner - The target user must currently be a member of the organization Note: This action cannot be used to remove the last owner of an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_AN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE_FROM_A_TEAM
nodes.name: Remove an organization role from a team
nodes.description: Revokes an organization role that a team currently possesses within an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_AN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE_FROM_A_USER
nodes.name: Remove an organization role from a user
nodes.description: Removes a custom organization role from a user within a GitHub organization, provided the organization exists, the user is a member, and the role_id corresponds to a valid custom role in that organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_APP_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Remove app access restrictions
nodes.description: Removes all GitHub App access restrictions from a protected branch in a repository; the branch must have protection rules configured, and this action does not alter user or team restrictions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_A_PROJECT_FROM_A_TEAM
nodes.name: Remove a project from a team
nodes.description: Removes a project from a team within an organization; this action requires the project to be currently associated with the team.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_A_REPOSITORY_COLLABORATOR
nodes.name: Remove a repository collaborator
nodes.description: Removes a collaborator from a specified GitHub repository, provided the repository exists and the user is an existing collaborator.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_A_REPOSITORY_FROM_AN_APP_INSTALLATION
nodes.name: Remove a repository from an app installation
nodes.description: Removes a repository from a GitHub App installation for the authenticated user, given a valid `installation_id` and the `repository_id` of a repository currently linked to that installation; this operation is idempotent.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_A_REPOSITORY_FROM_A_TEAM
nodes.name: Remove a repository from a team
nodes.description: Disassociates a repository from a team; team members may lose access permissions, but the repository and team are not deleted.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_A_SELECTED_REPOSITORY_FROM_A_USER_SECRET
nodes.name: Remove a selected repository from a user secret
nodes.description: Removes a selected repository's access to a user's Codespaces secret; the secret must exist and the repository must have previously been granted access.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_ASSIGNEES_FROM_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: Remove assignees from an issue
nodes.description: Removes specified assignees from a GitHub issue; requires push access, and invalid removal attempts are silently ignored.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_CUSTOM_LABEL_FROM_REPO_RUNNER
nodes.name: Remove custom label from repo runner
nodes.description: Removes a custom label from a repository's self-hosted runner; this operation is idempotent.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_CUSTOM_LABEL_FROM_SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER
nodes.name: Remove custom label from self hosted runner
nodes.description: Removes a currently assigned custom label (`name`) from a self-hosted runner (`runner_id`) in an organization (`org`). This action allows the organization to manage the labels associated with their runners effectively.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_CUSTOM_LABELS_FROM_SELF_HOSTED_REPO_RUNNER
nodes.name: Remove custom labels from self hosted repo runner
nodes.description: Removes all custom labels from a self-hosted runner registered at the repository level, leaving only its default labels and any labels inherited from its runner group or organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_INTERACTION_RESTRICTIONS_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Remove interaction restrictions for an organization
nodes.description: Removes all interaction restrictions from public repositories in the specified GitHub organization, allowing all users to resume interactions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_INTERACTION_RESTRICTIONS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Remove interaction restrictions for a repository
nodes.description: Removes all interaction restrictions for a repository, enabling all users to comment, open issues, and create pull requests by lifting any existing temporary interaction limits.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_ORG_DEV_ENV_SECRET_BY_NAME
nodes.name: Remove org dev env secret by name
nodes.description: Deletes a GitHub Codespaces secret from an organization by its name; this operation is idempotent.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_ORG_SECRET_BY_NAME
nodes.name: Remove org secret by name
nodes.description: Permanently removes a specific Dependabot secret, by its `secret_name`, from the GitHub `org`, making it unavailable to Dependabot for that organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_OUTSIDE_COLLABORATOR_FROM_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Remove outside collaborator from an organization
nodes.description: Removes a user, who must be an outside collaborator, from the specified GitHub organization, revoking their access to all its repositories.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_PUBLIC_ORG_MEMBERSHIP
nodes.name: Remove public org membership
nodes.description: Removes the authenticated user's public organization membership, making it private. The user remains a member of the organization but their membership is no longer publicly visible. This operation is idempotent - calling it when the membership is already private will succeed.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_PUBLIC_REPO_INTERACTION_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Remove user public repo interaction restrictions
nodes.description: Removes all interaction restrictions (limitations on comments, issues, or pull requests) from all public repositories owned by the authenticated user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_REPO_FROM_ORG_DEV_ENV_SECRET
nodes.name: Remove repository from organization dev environment secret
nodes.description: Removes a repository's access to an organization-level Codespaces secret, if it was previously granted.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_REPO_FROM_ORG_SECRET_WITH_SELECTED_VISIBILITY
nodes.name: Remove selected repo from org secret
nodes.description: Revokes a specific repository's access to an organization-level Dependabot secret, applicable only when the secret has 'selected' visibility and the specified repository currently has access.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_REQUESTED_REVIEWERS_FROM_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Remove requested reviewers from a pull request
nodes.description: Removes currently assigned user logins and/or team slugs from a GitHub pull request's list of requested reviewers.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_SELECTED_REPO_FROM_ORG_SECRET
nodes.name: Remove selected repository from an organization secret
nodes.description: Removes a specific repository's access to an organization-level secret; the repository must have been previously granted access to this secret.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_SELECTED_REPO_FROM_ORG_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Remove selected repository from an organization variable
nodes.description: Removes a repository's access to an organization variable that has 'selected' visibility. This endpoint removes a specific repository from an organization variable's access list. The variable must have its visibility set to 'selected' for this operation to work. Returns a 409 Conflict error if the variable visibility is not 'selected'.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_STATUS_CHECK_CONTEXTS
nodes.name: Remove status check contexts
nodes.description: Removes specified status check contexts from a protected branch's required status checks. This action removes the specified status check contexts from the existing list of required status checks for a protected branch. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with status checks configured. Note: The 'contexts' parameter is deprecated by GitHub in favor of 'checks' array. For new implementations, consider using update_status_check_protection with the 'checks' parameter instead.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_STATUS_CHECK_PROTECTION
nodes.name: Remove status check protection
nodes.description: Removes status check protection from a branch, disabling required status checks for merging pull requests; will only have an effect if status check protection is currently configured on the branch.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_TEAM_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Remove team access restrictions
nodes.description: Removes specified teams from push access to a protected branch. This action removes teams from the list of teams with push access to a protected branch. Removing a team will also remove push access for any child teams. Prerequisites: - The repository must be owned by an organization (not a personal account) - The branch must have protection rules enabled - The branch protection must have restrictions configured - The teams must currently have push access to the branch

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_TEAM_MEMBERSHIP_FOR_A_USER
nodes.name: Remove team membership for a user
nodes.description: Removes a user from a specific team within an organization. This action requires admin:org scope permissions. It will fail if: - Team synchronization with an Identity Provider (IdP) is enabled (403 Forbidden) - The organization, team, or user membership doesn't exist (404 Not Found) - You don't have sufficient permissions to manage team memberships (403 Forbidden) Note: If the removed user was the last member and the team is not nested, the team may be deleted automatically.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_USER_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Remove user access restrictions
nodes.description: Removes specified users from the list of people allowed to push to a protected branch. Important notes: - This action only works on organization-owned repositories (not personal repos) - The branch must have protection rules with push restrictions enabled - Users being removed must currently have push access to the branch - The combined total of users, apps, and teams is limited to 100 items

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_USER_AS_A_COLLABORATOR
nodes.name: Remove user as a collaborator (classic project)
nodes.description: Removes a user as a collaborator from an organization project (classic). IMPORTANT: This action uses the Projects (classic) REST API which is being deprecated. The Projects (classic) feature was sunset on GitHub.com. This API may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server versions before 3.17. Requirements: - You must be an organization owner or a project admin to remove a collaborator - The user must already be a collaborator on the specified project - The project must be an organization project (classic), not a Projects V2 project For GitHub.com users: Consider using GitHub Projects V2 instead, which can be managed through the GraphQL API.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REMOVE_USERS_CODESPACES_ACCESS_ORG
nodes.name: Remove users from codespaces access for an organization
nodes.description: Removes selected users from GitHub Codespaces billing access for an existing organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RENAME_A_BRANCH
nodes.name: Rename a branch
nodes.description: Renames an existing branch in a GitHub repository; the new name must be unique and adhere to GitHub's naming conventions, and the current branch name cannot contain wildcard characters.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RENDER_A_MARKDOWN_DOCUMENT
nodes.name: Render a markdown document
nodes.description: Renders Markdown to HTML; for 'gfm' mode, provide 'context' (owner/repo) to correctly link issues, pull requests, and user mentions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RENDER_MARKDOWN_RAW
nodes.name: Render Markdown (Raw Mode)
nodes.description: Tool to render raw Markdown text to HTML using GitHub's markdown API. Use when you need to convert plain Markdown content to HTML without GitHub-specific features like issue references or @mentions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REPLACE_ALL_REPOSITORY_TOPICS
nodes.name: Replace all repository topics
nodes.description: Replaces all topics of a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REPLACE_ORG_SECRET_VISIBILITY_TO_SELECTED
nodes.name: Replace org secret visibility to selected
nodes.description: Replaces the list of repositories that can access a Dependabot organization secret when the secret's visibility is already set to 'selected'.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REPLACE_REPO_ACCESS_ON_ORG_DEV_ENV_SECRET_SET
nodes.name: Replace repository access for an org Codespaces secret
nodes.description: Replaces the list of repositories that can access an organization-level Codespaces secret. This operation completely replaces the existing list of repositories with the provided list. The secret must already have its visibility set to 'selected' for this operation to succeed. Use this when you want to set an exact list of repositories that should have access. Prerequisites: - The organization must exist and you must have admin:org scope - The Codespaces secret must already exist in the organization - The secret's visibility must be set to 'selected' (not 'all' or 'private') Returns HTTP 204 No Content on success, 404 if secret/org not found, 409 if visibility is not 'selected'.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REQUEST_A_GITHUB_PAGES_BUILD
nodes.name: Request a github pages build
nodes.description: Manually triggers a GitHub Pages build for a repository if GitHub Pages is enabled, useful for deployments not automatically built or for retrying failed builds.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REQUEST_REVIEWERS_FOR_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Add reviewers to a specific pull request
nodes.description: Adds reviewers to a specific pull request identified by owner, repo, and pull_number. This is NOT a search action - it operates on an already-known PR number. Requires `owner`, `repo`, `pull_number`, and at least one of `reviewers` or `team_reviewers`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REREQUEST_A_CHECK_RUN
nodes.name: Rerequest a check run
nodes.description: Triggers a re-run of a specific check run in a GitHub repository, resetting its status to 'queued', clearing its conclusion, and triggering the `check_run` webhook with `rerequested` action. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'checks:write' permission. The check run can ONLY be re-requested by the same GitHub App that originally created it. OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (PAT) cannot use this endpoint.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REREQUEST_A_CHECK_SUITE
nodes.name: Rerequest a check suite
nodes.description: Triggers a new run of an existing check suite within a repository, resetting its status to 'queued', clearing its conclusion, and triggering the `check_suite` webhook with `rerequested` action. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'checks:write' permission. OAuth tokens and classic personal access tokens (PAT) cannot use this endpoint. Only GitHub App user access tokens, GitHub App installation access tokens, or fine-grained personal access tokens with checks:write permission are supported.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RE_RUN_A_JOB_FROM_A_WORKFLOW_RUN
nodes.name: Re-run a job from a workflow run
nodes.description: Re-runs a specific job and any dependent jobs from a GitHub Actions workflow run in the specified repository, optionally enabling debug logging.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RE_RUN_A_WORKFLOW
nodes.name: Rerun a workflow
nodes.description: Re-runs a specific GitHub Actions workflow run identified by its owner, repository, and run ID, optionally enabling debug logging.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RE_RUN_FAILED_JOBS_FROM_A_WORKFLOW_RUN
nodes.name: Rerun failed jobs from a workflow run
nodes.description: Re-runs all failed jobs and their dependent jobs from a specified workflow run if the run contains previously failed jobs.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RESET_A_TOKEN
nodes.name: Reset a token
nodes.description: Invalidates the provided OAuth `access_token` and generates a new one for the GitHub OAuth App identified by `client_id`. Used for token rotation when a token may be compromised or for regular security maintenance. Note: This endpoint requires the OAuth App owner's credentials (client_id/client_secret for Basic Auth).

nodes.label: GITHUB_RESTORE_A_PACKAGE_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Restore a package for an organization
nodes.description: Restores a package in an organization, provided it was deleted within the last 30 days.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RESTORE_A_PACKAGE_FOR_A_USER
nodes.name: Restore a package for a user
nodes.description: Restores a user-owned package previously deleted from GitHub Packages, if restorable under GitHub's data retention policy (typically within 30 days of deletion).

nodes.label: GITHUB_RESTORE_A_PACKAGE_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Restore a package for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Restores a package deleted by the authenticated user within the last 30 days, if its namespace and version are still available.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RESTORE_A_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Restore a package version for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Restores a package version that was deleted by the authenticated user within the last 30 days.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RESTORE_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Restore package version for an organization
nodes.description: Restores a package version for an organization, provided it was deleted within the last 30 days.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RESTORE_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_A_USER
nodes.name: Restore package version for a user
nodes.description: Restores a specific, user-owned package version if it was deleted within the last 30 days and its original namespace and version name are still available.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RETIRE_SPONSORS_TIER
nodes.name: Retire Sponsors Tier
nodes.description: Tool to retire a published payment tier from your GitHub Sponsors profile using GraphQL. Use when you need to prevent a tier from being used for new sponsorships. Existing sponsors at this tier remain unaffected.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REVIEW_ACCESS_WITH_PERSONAL_TOKEN
nodes.name: Review access with personal token
nodes.description: Approves or denies a pending fine-grained personal access token request for organization resources; must be performed by a GitHub App.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REVIEW_DEPLOYMENT_PROTECTION_RULES
nodes.name: Review Custom Deployment Rules For Workflow Run
nodes.description: Approves or rejects pending custom deployment protection rules for a workflow run. This endpoint allows GitHub Apps to review their own pending custom deployment protection rules for a specific workflow run. The review can either approve (allowing the deployment to proceed) or reject (blocking the deployment). **Important notes:** - GitHub Apps can only review their own custom deployment protection rules - This endpoint requires GitHub App installation access tokens with Deployments permission (write) - Custom deployment protection rules are available in public repos for all plans; for private/internal repos, GitHub Enterprise is required - Returns 204 No Content on success

nodes.label: GITHUB_REVIEW_PENDING_DEPLOYMENTS_FOR_A_WORKFLOW_RUN
nodes.name: Review pending deployments for a workflow run
nodes.description: Approves or rejects pending deployments for a specific workflow run that are in a 'waiting' state within specified, configured environments.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REVIEW_RESOURCE_REQUESTS_WITH_FINE_GRAINED_TOKENS
nodes.name: Review PAT Requests in Bulk
nodes.description: Approves or denies multiple fine-grained personal access token requests for an organization in bulk. This endpoint processes multiple PAT request approvals or denials in a single API call. All specified pat_request_ids must refer to currently pending requests. Important: This endpoint can ONLY be called by GitHub Apps with the 'organization_personal_access_token_requests:write' permission. It cannot be called with personal access tokens or OAuth apps. On success, returns HTTP 202 Accepted with an empty body.

nodes.label: GITHUB_REVOKE_AN_INSTALLATION_ACCESS_TOKEN
nodes.name: Revoke an installation access token
nodes.description: Revokes the GitHub App's current installation access token, immediately invalidating it for API authentication. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a GitHub App installation access token (ghs_* prefix) for authentication. OAuth tokens (gho_*) or personal access tokens are not supported and will result in a 403 Forbidden error. After successful revocation, the token becomes permanently invalid and cannot be used for any subsequent API calls. To continue API operations, a new installation access token must be generated.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RUN_GRAPH_QL_QUERY
nodes.name: Run GraphQL Query
nodes.description: Tool to run an arbitrary GitHub GraphQL v4 query or mutation. Use when fetching multiple datasets in one batch. Cost-based rate limit: ~5,000 points/hour; keep field selections narrow, avoid deep nesting, and include rateLimit in responses to monitor quota. Responses are nested under data.data (e.g., data.data.repository.vulnerabilityAlerts); always inspect the errors array even on HTTP 200, as partial failures embed there. Paginate by looping on pageInfo.hasNextPage and advancing with pageInfo.endCursor. Search results cap at ~1,000 items per query; split by label, date range, or repository for full coverage. Use ProjectV2 nodes, not Classic Projects (deprecated). Parallel mutations (e.g., addProjectV2ItemById) can trigger transient conflicts; use sequential execution.

nodes.label: GITHUB_RUN_GRAPH_QL_RELAY_QUERY
nodes.name: Run GraphQL Relay Query
nodes.description: Tool to run a GitHub GraphQL relay query. The relay field is a workaround for re-exposing the root query object. Use when you need to access root query fields through the relay context.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SEARCH_CODE
nodes.name: Search code
nodes.description: Searches code file contents and paths on the default branch of GitHub repositories using a query string; searches only files under 384KB, returns max 1000 results by best match, and is optimized for precision.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SEARCH_CODE_ALL_PAGES
nodes.name: Search code (all pages)
nodes.description: Tool to search code across multiple pages using GitHub's code search API. Use when single-page searches may miss matches and you need a full or capped result set. GitHub caps results at ~1,000 total items regardless of pagination; results for broad queries may be silently truncated. Only the default branch is indexed, and very large files may be excluded — treat results as potentially partial. Rate limit: ~30 requests/minute; honor `Retry-After` on 403/429 responses.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SEARCH_COMMITS
nodes.name: Search commits
nodes.description: Finds commits on GitHub using a query string (q) supporting keywords and qualifiers, with options for sorting and pagination.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SEARCH_COMMITS_BY_AUTHOR
nodes.name: Search commits by author across repos
nodes.description: Searches commits across GitHub using the Search API (e.g., by author/email/org/date range) without per-repo scanning. Use when you need to find commits by author, committer email, organization, or date ranges across multiple repositories. This avoids the need to list commits per repository and prevents 409 errors on empty repos.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SEARCH_GITHUB_GRAPHQL
nodes.name: GitHub GraphQL Search
nodes.description: Tool to perform GitHub GraphQL searches across issues, pull requests, repositories, users, and discussions. Returns up to 1,000 results with cursor-based pagination. Use when you need structured search results with specific fields.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SEARCH_ISSUES_AND_PULL_REQUESTS
nodes.name: Search issues and pull requests
nodes.description: Searches GitHub for issues and pull requests. Supports keywords, qualifiers (repo:, org:, user:, state:, label:, author:, assignee:, mentions:, etc.), and type filters (is:issue, is:pr). Type filters cannot be used alone - they must be combined with at least one keyword or other qualifier. All other qualifiers work independently. The @me shorthand (e.g., assignee:@me, mentions:@me) is automatically resolved to your username. Logical operators (AND, OR, NOT) are supported but limited to a maximum of 5 operators total per query.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SEARCH_LABELS
nodes.name: Search labels
nodes.description: Searches for labels within a GitHub repository by keywords in their names or descriptions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SEARCH_REPOSITORIES
nodes.name: Search repositories
nodes.description: Searches GitHub repositories using a flexible query (keywords, qualifiers) with sorting, ordering, and pagination. IMPORTANT: The 'q' parameter (search query) is REQUIRED and must always be provided.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SEARCH_TOPICS
nodes.name: Search topics
nodes.description: Finds topics on GitHub using keywords and qualifiers with GitHub's search syntax, supporting pagination.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SEARCH_USERS
nodes.name: Search users
nodes.description: Searches for users on GitHub by criteria like username, email, location, followers, or repository associations, using a flexible query string `q`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_ADMIN_BRANCH_PROTECTION
nodes.name: Set admin branch protection
nodes.description: Enables administrator enforcement on a branch, making existing protection rules also apply to administrators; branch protection rules must already be configured.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_APP_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Set app access restrictions
nodes.description: Replaces the list of GitHub Apps permitted to push to a protected branch. This action completely replaces the existing list of apps with push access - it does not add to or merge with the current list. Requirements: - The branch must already have protection rules enabled with push restrictions configured - Apps must be installed on the repository and have 'contents' write permission - Push restrictions are only available for organization-owned repositories with GitHub Team or Enterprise Cloud plans

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_A_REPOSITORY_SUBSCRIPTION
nodes.name: Set a repository subscription
nodes.description: Sets the authenticated user's notification subscription for a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_A_THREAD_SUBSCRIPTION
nodes.name: Set a thread subscription
nodes.description: Sets whether a GitHub notification thread is ignored (muted) or unignored (unmuted), for a `thread_id` that must identify an existing notification thread.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_DEFAULT_WORKFLOW_PERMISSIONS_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Set default workflow permissions for an organization
nodes.description: Updates an organization's default GITHUB_TOKEN permissions for workflows and whether GitHub Actions can approve pull requests; note that allowing Actions to approve pull requests (`can_approve_pull_request_reviews: true`) is a security risk.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_DEFAULT_WORKFLOW_PERMISSIONS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Set default workflow permissions for a repository
nodes.description: Sets the default permissions for the GITHUB_TOKEN within a repository and configures whether GitHub Actions can approve pull requests.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_EMAIL_VISIBILITY
nodes.name: Set primary email visibility
nodes.description: Sets the visibility ('public' or 'private') of the authenticated user's primary email address on GitHub, if one is configured.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_GITHUB_ACTIONS_PERMISSIONS_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization
nodes.description: Sets the GitHub Actions permissions policy for an organization, specifying which repositories can run Actions and which actions/workflows are allowed; if 'selected' is chosen for either, manage the specific lists via other endpoints.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_GITHUB_ACTIONS_PERMISSIONS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Set github actions permissions for a repository
nodes.description: Sets GitHub Actions permissions for a repository, enabling/disabling Actions and defining the policy for allowed actions and reusable workflows.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_INTERACTION_RESTRICTIONS_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Set interaction restrictions for an organization
nodes.description: Limits interactions (comments, new issues, PRs) in an organization's public repositories by user type and duration, typically to mitigate high traffic or unwanted activity.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_INTERACTION_RESTRICTIONS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Set interaction restrictions for a repository
nodes.description: Temporarily limits which GitHub users (e.g., existing_users, contributors_only) can interact (comment, open issues, create pull requests) in a repository for a specified duration.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_INTERACTION_RESTRICTIONS_FOR_YOUR_PUBLIC_REPOS
nodes.name: Set interaction restrictions for your public repositories
nodes.description: Sets or updates temporary interaction restrictions for all public repositories owned by the authenticated user, overriding any repository-specific limits.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_LABELS_FOR_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: Set labels for an issue
nodes.description: Replaces all existing labels on a GitHub issue with a new set of labels.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_OIDC_SUBJECT_CLAIM_TEMPLATE
nodes.name: Set custom OIDC subject claim template
nodes.description: Sets the OpenID Connect (OIDC) subject claim template for a GitHub repository, allowing use of the default template or a custom one defined by `include_claim_keys` if `use_default` is `false`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_ORG_ALLOWED_ACTIONS
nodes.name: Set allowed actions and workflows for an organization
nodes.description: Sets the GitHub Actions permissions for an existing organization, specifying allowed GitHub-owned actions, verified creator actions, and action/workflow patterns from public repositories.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_ORG_RUNNER_LABELS
nodes.name: Set custom labels for a self-hosted runner for an organization
nodes.description: Sets the custom labels for a self-hosted runner in an organization; this operation does not affect default system-assigned labels (e.g., 'self-hosted', 'linux', 'x64').

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_REPO_ALLOWED_ACTIONS
nodes.name: Set repo allowed actions
nodes.description: Sets allowed GitHub Actions and reusable workflows for a repository, managing permissions for GitHub-owned, verified creator, or specific pattern-matched actions/workflows (note: `patterns_allowed` applies only to public repositories).

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_REPO_RUNNER_LABELS
nodes.name: Set custom labels for repo runner
nodes.description: Replaces all custom labels for a specific self-hosted runner in a repository; an empty list for `labels` removes all existing custom labels.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_SELECTED_REPOS_FOR_ORG_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Set selected repositories for an organization variable
nodes.description: Replaces the list of repositories that can access an organization-level variable; the variable's visibility must be 'selected'.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_SELECTED_REPOSITORIES_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION_SECRET
nodes.name: Set selected repositories for an organization secret
nodes.description: Replaces the list of repositories that can access an organization secret; only effective if the secret's visibility is 'selected'. Prerequisites: - The organization secret must already exist - The secret's visibility must be set to 'selected' (not 'all' or 'private') - Requires admin:org scope or actions secrets fine-grained permission This action completely replaces the repository access list. To add or remove individual repositories without affecting others, use the dedicated add/remove repository actions instead.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_SELECTED_REPOSITORIES_FOR_A_USER_SECRET
nodes.name: Set selected repositories for a user secret
nodes.description: Defines the list of repositories permitted to access a specific Codespaces secret for the authenticated user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_STATUS_CHECK_CONTEXTS
nodes.name: Set status check contexts
nodes.description: Replaces required status check contexts for a protected branch, requiring admin permissions; an empty `contexts` array removes all checks.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_TEAM_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Set team access restrictions
nodes.description: Replaces the list of teams with push access to a protected branch. This action sets (replaces) the teams that have push access to a protected branch. Unlike 'Add team access restrictions' (POST), this action replaces the entire list rather than adding to it. Use an empty list to remove all team restrictions. Prerequisites: - The repository must be owned by an organization (not a personal account) - The branch must have protection rules enabled - The branch protection must have restrictions configured - The teams must exist in the organization and have repository access

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_USER_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS
nodes.name: Set user access restrictions
nodes.description: Replaces the list of users with push access to a protected branch in an organization repository. Important notes: - This action only works on organization-owned repositories (not personal repos) - The branch must already have protection rules with restrictions enabled - This REPLACES (not adds to) the existing list of users with push access - Users must have write access to the repository to be added - The combined total of users, apps, and teams is limited to 100 items - To add users without replacing, use the 'add_user_access_restrictions' action instead

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_USER_INTERACTION_LIMIT_GRAPH_QL
nodes.name: Set User Interaction Limit (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to set interaction limits on a user's public repositories using GraphQL. Use when you need to temporarily restrict who can comment, open issues, or create pull requests. Limits apply to the authenticated user or a specified user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SET_WORKFLOW_EXTERNAL_ACCESS
nodes.name: Set workflow access level outside repository
nodes.description: Sets the access level for workflows outside a repository to use actions and reusable workflows within that repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_STAR_A_GIST
nodes.name: Star a gist
nodes.description: Stars a GitHub gist identified by `gist_id`; this action is idempotent and returns a 204 No Content status upon success, even if the gist is already starred.

nodes.label: GITHUB_STAR_A_REPOSITORY_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Star a specified repository (requires owner and repo)
nodes.description: Stars a specified repository (identified by owner and repo name) for the authenticated user; this action is idempotent and succeeds even if the repository is already starred. Requires explicit repository identification via owner and repo parameters.

nodes.label: GITHUB_START_A_CODESPACE_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Start a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Initiates the startup process for an existing GitHub Codespace (identified by `codespace_name`) if it's in a startable state like 'Available' or 'Stopped'.

nodes.label: GITHUB_STOP_A_CODESPACE_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION_USER
nodes.name: Stop a codespace for an organization user
nodes.description: Stops a running codespace for an organization member. This action allows organization administrators to stop a codespace that belongs to a member of their organization. The codespace must be in a running state (e.g., 'Available') to be stopped. Required permissions: - OAuth/PAT: admin:org scope - Fine-grained tokens: 'Organization codespaces' (write) or 'Codespaces lifecycle admin' (write) Common HTTP responses: - 200: Codespace successfully stopped, returns codespace object - 304: Not modified (codespace already stopped) - 401: Authentication required - 403: Insufficient permissions (need admin:org) - 404: Organization, user, or codespace not found

nodes.label: GITHUB_STOP_A_CODESPACE_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER
nodes.name: Stop a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Stops a running or available Codespace for the authenticated user, pausing its execution and billing.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SUBMIT_A_REVIEW_FOR_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Submit a review for a pull request
nodes.description: Finalizes a pending pull request review (identified by `review_id`) with a required `event` (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, COMMENT) and an optional `body`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_SYNC_FORK_BRANCH_WITH_UPSTREAM
nodes.name: Sync a fork branch with the upstream repository
nodes.description: Synchronizes a branch in a forked GitHub repository with its upstream counterpart, assuming the repository is a fork, the branch exists, an upstream is configured, and the merge is conflict-free.

nodes.label: GITHUB_TEST_REPOSITORY_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Test the push repository webhook
nodes.description: Triggers a simulated push event to test a repository's push webhook; a test event is only delivered if the webhook is subscribed to 'push' events, otherwise, it returns 204 No Content without sending a POST.

nodes.label: GITHUB_TRANSFER_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Transfer a repository
nodes.description: Initiates a repository transfer to a new owner (who must accept the request); if the new owner is an organization, it must be configured to allow transfers.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UNBLOCK_A_USER
nodes.name: Unblock a user
nodes.description: Unblocks a GitHub user, provided they are currently blocked by the authenticated user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UNBLOCK_USER_FROM_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Unblock a user from an organization
nodes.description: Unblocks a user from an organization, allowing renewed interaction with its resources, provided the user is currently blocked (otherwise, a 404 error may occur).

nodes.label: GITHUB_UNFOLLOW_A_USER
nodes.name: Unfollow a user
nodes.description: Unfollows an existing GitHub user; this action is idempotent, succeeding even if the authenticated user is not currently following them.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UNFOLLOW_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Unfollow organization
nodes.description: Tool to unfollow a GitHub organization using GraphQL. Use when you need to stop following a specific organization on GitHub.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UNFOLLOW_USER
nodes.name: Unfollow user
nodes.description: Tool to unfollow a GitHub user using GraphQL. Use when you need to stop following a specific user on GitHub.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UNLOCK_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: Unlock an issue
nodes.description: Unlocks a currently locked GitHub issue in the specified repository, allowing new comments and interactions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UNLOCK_ORGANIZATION_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Unlock an organization repository
nodes.description: Unlocks a repository that was locked for migration within a GitHub organization. This action is part of the organization migrations workflow. When you start a migration with `lock_repositories=true`, the source repositories are locked to prevent changes during export. After the migration is complete and applied to the target instance, use this action to unlock the repositories so they can be accessed or deleted. Requirements: - You must be an organization owner to unlock repositories - The repository must be currently locked as part of the specified migration - This endpoint does not work with GitHub App tokens or fine-grained personal access tokens Typical workflow: 1. Start an organization migration with POST /orgs/{org}/migrations 2. Wait for the migration to complete (state: "exported") 3. Apply the migration to your target instance 4. Unlock each migrated repository using this action 5. Delete the source repositories if no longer needed

nodes.label: GITHUB_UNLOCK_USER_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Unlock a user repository
nodes.description: Unlocks a repository (`repo_name`) that was locked as part of a user migration (`migration_id`), making it usable or deletable; this action requires the repository to be currently locked.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UNSTAR_A_GIST
nodes.name: Unstar a gist
nodes.description: Removes a star from the specified gist; the action is idempotent and will not error if the gist was not previously starred by the user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UNSTAR_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Unstar a repository
nodes.description: Removes the authenticated user's star from a specified repository, which must already be starred by the user.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_CHECK_RUN
nodes.name: Update a check run
nodes.description: Updates an existing check run for a specific commit in a repository, allowing modifications to its status, conclusion, output, and other details. NOTE: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'checks:write' permission - OAuth tokens and personal access tokens cannot update check runs.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_CODE_SCANNING_ALERT
nodes.name: Update a code scanning alert
nodes.description: Updates a specific code scanning alert in a GitHub repository, primarily to change its state (e.g., 'open' or 'dismissed').

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_COMMIT_COMMENT
nodes.name: Update a commit comment
nodes.description: Changes the body of an existing commit comment.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_CUSTOM_ORGANIZATION_ROLE
nodes.name: Update a custom organization role
nodes.description: Updates an existing custom role in an organization by modifying its name, description, or permissions; at least one of these fields must be provided.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_DEPLOYMENT_BRANCH_POLICY
nodes.name: Update a deployment branch policy
nodes.description: Updates the name pattern of an existing deployment branch policy for a specific environment in a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_DISCUSSION
nodes.name: Update a discussion
nodes.description: Updates the title and/or body of a specific team discussion within an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_DISCUSSION_COMMENT
nodes.name: Update a discussion comment
nodes.description: Updates the body of a comment in a team's discussion within an organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_GIST
nodes.name: Update a gist
nodes.description: Updates a gist's description, and/or its files (including content, filename changes, or deletion).

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_GIST_COMMENT
nodes.name: Update a gist comment
nodes.description: Updates an existing comment on a specified gist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_LABEL
nodes.name: Update a label
nodes.description: Updates an existing label's name, color, or description within a specified repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_MILESTONE
nodes.name: Update a milestone
nodes.description: Updates a milestone in a repository, identified by `owner`, `repo`, and `milestone_number`, by allowing modification of its `title`, `state`, `description`, or `due_on`; at least one of these four attributes must be provided to perform an update.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_AN_ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Update an environment variable
nodes.description: Updates an existing environment variable's name and/or value in a specific GitHub repository environment; requires providing either a new name or a new value.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_AN_ISSUE
nodes.name: Update an issue
nodes.description: Updates an existing GitHub issue's title, body, state, milestone, labels, or assignees; `state_reason` is only processed if `state` also changes, and use `null` or `[]` to clear applicable fields.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_AN_ISSUE_COMMENT
nodes.name: Update an issue comment
nodes.description: Updates an existing comment on an issue or pull request within a specified repository. You can only update comments that the authenticated user has permission to edit. The comment_id can be obtained from the list_issue_comments or get_an_issue_comment actions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_AN_ORGANIZATION
nodes.name: Update an organization
nodes.description: Updates an organization's settings; changing security-related fields requires admin, owner, or security manager permissions.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_AN_ORGANIZATION_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Update an organization variable
nodes.description: Updates an existing GitHub Actions organization variable's name, value, or visibility (`all`, `private`, `selected`), requiring `selected_repository_ids` with valid repository IDs if visibility is `selected`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_AN_ORGANIZATION_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Update an organization webhook
nodes.description: Updates the configuration (URL, content type, secret, SSL verification), subscribed events, active status, or name of an existing webhook for a specified organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PROJECT
nodes.name: Update a project
nodes.description: Updates an existing GitHub project (V2) attributes like title, description, visibility, and state. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed. At least one field to update must be provided.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST
nodes.name: Update a pull request
nodes.description: Updates an existing pull request, allowing changes to attributes like title, body, state, base branch, and maintainer modification settings.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST_BRANCH
nodes.name: Update a pull request branch
nodes.description: Updates an open pull request's head branch by merging the latest changes from its base branch, if mergeable and repository merging is enabled; operates asynchronously.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REFERENCE
nodes.name: Update a reference
nodes.description: Updates an existing Git reference (e.g., a branch or tag) to point to a specific commit SHA. By default, only allows fast-forward updates (where the new commit is a descendant of the current one). Set `force=true` to allow non-fast-forward updates that rewrite history. IMPORTANT: The reference must already exist - use 'Create a reference' action to create new refs.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_RELEASE
nodes.name: Update a release
nodes.description: Updates an existing release in a GitHub repository, allowing modification of its attributes; if linking a discussion, the `discussion_category_name` must refer to an existing category in the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_RELEASE_ASSET
nodes.name: Update a release asset
nodes.description: Updates the name, label, or state of a release asset in a GitHub repository, requiring at least one of these properties to be provided for modification.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REPOSITORY
nodes.name: Update a repository
nodes.description: Updates settings for an existing GitHub repository, such as name, description, visibility, merge strategies, and security configurations.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REPOSITORY_INVITATION
nodes.name: Update a repository invitation
nodes.description: Updates an active repository invitation to modify the invited user's permissions; the specified repository and invitation must exist.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REPOSITORY_RULESET
nodes.name: Update a repository ruleset
nodes.description: Updates an existing repository ruleset, identified by `ruleset_id` for a given repository, allowing partial updates to its configuration such as name, target, enforcement, bypass actors, conditions, and rules.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REPOSITORY_VARIABLE
nodes.name: Update a repository variable
nodes.description: Updates the name and/or value of an existing GitHub Actions variable in a repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REPOSITORY_WEBHOOK
nodes.name: Update a repository webhook
nodes.description: Updates the URL, content type, secret, SSL verification, events, or active status for an existing repository webhook, specified by `owner`, `repo`, and `hook_id`.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_A_TEAM
nodes.name: Update a team
nodes.description: Updates a team's settings (e.g., name, description, privacy, parent team) within a GitHub organization, identified by its slug and organization name.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_BRANCH_PROTECTION
nodes.name: Update branch protection
nodes.description: Updates the protection settings for an existing branch in a repository, which must not contain wildcard characters.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_CODE_SCANNING_DEFAULT_SETUP
nodes.name: Update a code scanning default setup configuration
nodes.description: Updates the default setup configuration for code scanning in a repository; GitHub Advanced Security must be enabled for the repository.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_CODESPACE
nodes.name: Update a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Updates an existing GitHub Codespace's machine type, display name, or recent folders for the authenticated user; machine type changes take effect on the next start.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_GIT_LFS_PREFERENCE
nodes.name: Update Git LFS Preference
nodes.description: Sets the Git Large File Storage (LFS) preference for a repository during a source import. NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_ISSUE_TYPE
nodes.name: Update issue type
nodes.description: Update an existing issue type for a GitHub organization. Requires the authenticated user to be an organization administrator. OAuth tokens need admin:org scope.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_ORGANIZATION_WEBHOOK_CONFIGURATION
nodes.name: Update a webhook configuration for an organization
nodes.description: Updates the configuration (URL, content type, secret, SSL verification) for an existing webhook within a specified organization.

nodes.label: GITHUB_UPDATE_PROJECT_CARD
nodes.name: Update an existing project card (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Updates an existing project card's note and/or archived status (GitHub Classic Projects). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com.
